message.content:
You are reading the REAL POLARIS codebase at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel. Do not speculate — open the files and quote them.

Find the EXACT code path that verifies a generated sentence against evidence spans and DROPS it if unsupported (strict_verify / B11 / span-grounding / kept_fraction / no_provenance_token). Read it properly. Report: where a sentence is admitted or killed, what the admission predicate is, and precisely what happens to a sentence that is an INFERENCE ACROSS TWO SOURCES (no single span supports it). Quote the predicate.

Ground everything in file:line. If something does not exist, say so plainly — that is a finding.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	
3	Per-sentence check enforcing:
4	  (a) at least one provenance token present
5	  (b) every token references an evidence_id in the pool
6	  (c) span bounds are valid (0 <= start <= end <= len(text))
7	  (d) every decimal in the sentence appears in the cited spans
8	  (e) sentence and span share >= MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP content words
9	  (f) I-bug-092: span semantically ENTAILS the sentence's specific claims
10	      (gated behind PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, defaults off)

12	Checks (a)-(e) are mechanical "provenance hygiene" — they bound a claim to
13	the same TOPIC as its span. Check (f) is "provenance correctness" — it
14	verifies the span actually entails the sentence's specific facts. The
15	audit on 2026-05-09 found that a capable generator can pass (a)-(e) by
16	producing topically-overlapping prose that introduces unsourced specifics
17	(e.g. inserting "pancreatic β-cells", "lipid metabolism", "energy storage"
18	into a span that says only "synergistic actions on insulin secretion,

22	constructs a VerifiedSentence for use by the generator orchestrator.

25	  PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP — minimum shared content words (default 2)
26	  PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT       — "off" | "warn" | "enforce" (default)

110	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
111	    """Lowercase content words (>=3 chars, not stopwords) from `text`.
112	
113	    I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): the Latin path below is byte-identical to the
114	    pre-L4 engine (English unchanged). It is UNIONED with script-aware tokens
115	    (CJK character bigrams + space-delimited non-Latin words) so the
116	    ``overlap_too_low`` floor counts real shared content on non-Latin claims
117	    instead of mis-counting ZERO. Reverts byte-identical under
118	    PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=0.
119	    """
120	    latin = {
121	        m.group(0).lower()
122	        for m in _WORD_RE.finditer(text)
123	        if len(m.group(0)) >= 3 and m.group(0).lower() not in _STOPWORDS
124	    }
125	    return latin | extra_script_tokens(text)

133	def _percents(text: str) -> set[str]:
134	    """I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2): PERCENT VALUES printed in `text` — the number
135	    immediately before "%"/"percent" (`_PERCENT_RE` group 1). "15%" and
136	    "15 percent" both yield "15"; a bare "15" (page number / year / count)
137	    yields nothing. Used to require a claim's printed percent to appear AS A
138	    PERCENT in a cited span, not merely as a coincidental bare digit."""
139	    return {m.group(1) for m in _PERCENT_RE.finditer(text)}

156	#
157	# Mirrors the Wave-2 PERCENT-role machinery: a strictly ADDITIVE, default-ON gate
158	# with a byte-identical-OFF kill-switch (PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0).
159	# It can only ADD a ``binding_qualifier_dropped`` drop — it never relaxes an
160	# existing check, so it stacks ON TOP of the I-faith-001 incumbent engine (that
161	# freeze covered engine REPLACEMENT, not an additive strengthening gate).
162	#
163	# WHY: the composer copies a numeral's VALUE but can silently drop the epistemic /
164	# scope qualifier bound to it in the cited span ("some estimates suggest 46% of
165	# jobs ... under a complementary-software scenario" restated as a flat "46% of
166	# jobs"). That is a certainty distortion (From-May-to-Is, arXiv:2606.07951): a
167	# hedged / conditional figure re-stated as a settled fact. The decimal / percent /
168	# overlap legs all PASS the stripped restatement, and the NLI leg is systematically
169	# lenient to hedge-dropping (a "more general" statement is still entailed), so the
170	# defect needs its OWN completeness gate — exactly as the P1-3 numeric-completeness
171	# guard mirrors the numeric leg.
172	#
173	# CALIBRATION (the over-fire guard): the gate anchors on a SUBSTANTIVE numeral
174	# (a decimal with a fractional part, or a percent-expressed value) that is SHARED
175	# between the span and the sentence, and only fires when an epistemic marker sits
176	# within a proximity window of that numeral IN THE SPAN while the sentence carries
177	# NO marker at all. Bare integers (page numbers, years, sample counts like the
178	# box1 negative ``N=1879``) are NOT substantive, so a plain short finding such as
179	# ``HbA1c reduced 2.3 points`` — whose span carries the SAME flat value with no
180	# nearby marker — never fires. The window bounds a marker to the numeral it
181	# actually qualifies; the broad SENTENCE-side marker set means any surviving hedge
182	# lets the sentence pass (the safe under-drop direction).

184	DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS = 12

189	# clinical spans). LAW VI: fully overridable via PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_LEXICON

212	def _qualifier_retention_enabled() -> bool:
213	    """I-deepfix-001 (P5). True (DEFAULT) => run the epistemic-qualifier RETENTION
214	    gate; False => skip it. Strictly faithfulness-TIGHTENING and strictly ADDITIVE

222	def _qualifier_proximity_tokens() -> int:
223	    """Proximity window (in whitespace tokens) within which a span marker is bound
224	    to a numeral. PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS (default 12). An
225	    unset / non-integer value falls back to the default; a negative value clamps to
226	    0 (the tightest window). 0 = same-token only."""
227	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS", "").strip()
228	    try:

234	def _qualifier_lexicon() -> tuple[str, ...]:
235	    """The active epistemic-marker lexicon. LAW VI: PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_LEXICON
236	    (comma-separated) overrides the curated default; an empty / all-blank override
237	    falls back to the default rather than disabling the gate silently."""
238	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_LEXICON", "").strip()
239	    if not raw:
240	        return _DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_LEXICON
241	    items = tuple(part.strip().lower() for part in raw.split(",") if part.strip())
242	    return items or _DEFAULT_QUALIFIER_LEXICON

245	@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=8)
246	def _qualifier_marker_re(lexicon: tuple[str, ...]) -> "re.Pattern[str]":
247	    """A word-boundary-anchored alternation over `lexicon`, longest phrase first so
248	    a multi-word marker ("up to") wins over its prefix. Internal spaces match any
249	    run of whitespace. Cached per distinct lexicon tuple."""
250	    parts = sorted({m for m in lexicon if m}, key=len, reverse=True)
251	    if not parts:
252	        # An all-blank lexicon can never match — a pattern that matches nothing.
253	        return re.compile(r"(?!x)x")
254	    alts = [re.escape(p).replace(r"\ ", r"\s+").replace(" ", r"\s+") for p in parts]
255	    return re.compile(r"\b(?:" + "|".join(alts) + r")\b", re.IGNORECASE)

258	def _has_epistemic_marker(text: str, marker_re: "re.Pattern[str]") -> bool:
259	    """True iff any epistemic marker appears in `text`."""
260	    return marker_re.search(text) is not None

263	def _substantive_numerals(text: str) -> set[str]:
264	    """Numerals carrying a CLAIMED magnitude: decimals with a fractional part, plus
265	    percent-expressed values (`_percents`). Bare integers — page numbers, years,
266	    sample counts ("N=1879") — are EXCLUDED: they are not the epistemically-hedged
267	    magnitudes the gate protects, and including them would over-fire on plain
268	    findings (the box1 calibration negatives)."""
269	    decimals_with_fraction = {n for n in _decimals(text) if "." in n}
270	    return decimals_with_fraction | _percents(text)
271

273	def _marker_binds_numeral_in_span(
274	    span_text: str,
275	    numeral: str,
276	    window: int,
277	    marker_re: "re.Pattern[str]",
278	) -> bool:
279	    """True iff an epistemic marker sits within `window` whitespace tokens of an
280	    occurrence of `numeral` inside `span_text`. The numeral is matched as a WHOLE
281	    number within a token ("46" matches "46%," / "46" but not "460" / "46.5"), so a
282	    coincidental substring never binds a marker."""
283	    tokens = span_text.split()
284	    for i, tok in enumerate(tokens):
285	        if numeral not in _decimals(tok):
286	            continue
287	        lo = max(0, i - window)
288	        hi = min(len(tokens), i + window + 1)
289	        if _has_epistemic_marker(" ".join(tokens[lo:hi]), marker_re):
290	            return True
291	    return False

386	def verify_sentence(
387	    sentence_text: str,
388	    pool: EvidencePool,
389	    min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
390	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
391	) -> tuple[bool, DropReason | None]:
392	    """Return (verifier_pass, drop_reason) for `sentence_text` against `pool`.
393	
394	    Pass: returns (True, None).
395	    Fail: returns (False, drop_reason) with reason from DropReason literal.
396	
397	    Implements CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3 in order:
398	      1. at least one well-formed token        → no_provenance_token
399	      2. every token references known source_id → invalid_token
400	      3. spans within source bounds            → span_out_of_range
401	      4. every decimal in sentence in spans    → numeric_mismatch
402	      5. >=N shared content words              → overlap_too_low
403	      6. (I-bug-092) span entails sentence     → entailment_failed
404	         (gated by PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, off by default)
405	
406	    If `is_synthesis_claim=True` AND the sentence has no tokens, return
407	    (True, None) — synthesis claims pass without provenance by definition
408	    (I-f5-006). If a synthesis claim DOES carry tokens, that's a generator
409	    bug; we still run token checks so the underlying invariant holds.
410	    """
411	    threshold = (
412	        min_content_overlap
413	        if min_content_overlap is not None
414	        else _min_overlap_threshold()
415	    )
416	
417	    tokens = extract_tokens(sentence_text)
418	    if not tokens:
419	        if is_synthesis_claim:
420	            return True, None
421	        return False, "no_provenance_token"
422	
423	    # Validate each token against the pool. Collect span texts for
424	    # later numeric + overlap checks.
425	    span_texts: list[str] = []
426	    for token in tokens:
427	        reason = validate_token_against_pool(token, pool)
428	        if reason == "invalid_token":
429	            return False, "invalid_token"
430	        if reason == "span_out_of_range":
431	            return False, "span_out_of_range"
432	        # Token valid — fetch its span text
433	        span = get_span_text(token, pool)
434	        if span is None:
435	            # Belt-and-suspenders: token validated but get_span_text said no.
436	            return False, "span_out_of_range"
437	        span_texts.append(span)
438	
439	    sentence_clean = strip_tokens(sentence_text)
440	    combined_span = " ".join(span_texts)
441	
442	    # Decimal match: every decimal in sentence must appear in the combined
443	    # span text. Permits the spans to contain MORE decimals than the
444	    # sentence cites — that's fine; the constraint is one-way.
445	    sentence_decimals = _decimals(sentence_clean)
446	    span_decimals = _decimals(combined_span)
447	    if not sentence_decimals.issubset(span_decimals):
448	        return False, "numeric_mismatch"
449	
450	    # I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2) PERCENT-ROLE re-check (parity with
451	    # provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance). A printed percent
452	    # ("15%", "15 percent") is a PERCENT claim: it must be carried by a cited
453	    # span AS A PERCENT, not merely as a bare digit that coincidentally equals
454	    # the value (a page number / year / count). The decimal check above passes a
455	    # bare "15" via the number union; this compares PERCENT-vs-PERCENT
456	    # (`_percents`, same regex both sides). Span percents are read PER cited span
457	    # text (not the joined string) so a "15" ending one span and a "%" starting
458	    # the next can never be glued into a spurious percent. STRICTLY ADDITIVE:
459	    # only ADDS a drop; nothing grounded by a genuine in-span percent is newly
460	    # dropped. Default-ON; PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH=0 reverts byte-identical.
461	    if _percent_role_match_enabled():
462	        sentence_percents = _percents(sentence_clean)
463	        if sentence_percents:
464	            span_percents: set[str] = set()
465	            for _span in span_texts:
466	                span_percents |= _percents(_span)
467	            if not sentence_percents.issubset(span_percents):
468	                return False, "percent_not_in_cited_span"
469	
470	    # I-deepfix-001 (P5) EPISTEMIC-QUALIFIER RETENTION re-check. The composer can
471	    # copy a numeral's VALUE but drop the hedge / scope qualifier the cited span
472	    # binds to it ("some estimates suggest 46% ... under a complementary-software
473	    # scenario" -> a flat "46%"). For each cited span, if a SUBSTANTIVE numeral in
474	    # the span (decimal / percent — never a bare count / year) also appears in the
475	    # sentence AND an epistemic marker sits within
476	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS of THAT numeral IN THE SPAN, the
477	    # sentence must itself carry a marker; else it dropped a binding qualifier ->
478	    # (False, "binding_qualifier_dropped"). Span percents/decimals are read PER
479	    # cited span (not the joined string) so a marker in one span can never bind a
480	    # numeral in another. STRICTLY ADDITIVE: only ADDS a drop; the verbatim K-span
481	    # fallback retains the qualifier by construction, so a genuine hedged finding
482	    # still ships. Default-ON; PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0 reverts
483	    # byte-identical. Read at call time.
484	    if _qualifier_retention_enabled():
485	        marker_re = _qualifier_marker_re(_qualifier_lexicon())
486	        if not _has_epistemic_marker(sentence_clean, marker_re):
487	            window = _qualifier_proximity_tokens()
488	            sentence_numbers = _decimals(sentence_clean)
489	            for _span in span_texts:
490	                shared = _substantive_numerals(_span) & sentence_numbers
491	                for numeral in shared:
492	                    if _marker_binds_numeral_in_span(
493	                        _span, numeral, window, marker_re
494	                    ):
495	                        return False, "binding_qualifier_dropped"
496	
497	    # I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): FAIL-CLOSED on unsegmentable script content. A
498	    # claim carrying a run of letters in a script we cannot segment
499	    # (Thai/Lao/Khmer/Myanmar/Tibetan) yields no content tokens; rather than let
500	    # it slip past the overlap floor on a coincidental decimal match, we drop it
501	    # — we cannot establish lexical grounding and MUST NOT guess a pass (the
502	    # lethal weakened-positive). CJK / Arabic / Cyrillic etc. tokenize correctly
503	    # via _content_words and never reach here. Reverts byte-identical under
504	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=0.
505	    if has_unsegmentable_content(sentence_clean):
506	        return False, "unsegmentable_script"
507	
508	    # Content-word overlap
509	    sentence_words = _content_words(sentence_clean)
510	    span_words = _content_words(combined_span)
511	    # BUG-03 (FX-02, #1106): a truly contentless sentence — NO content words AND no decimals —
512	    # is dropped EXPLICITLY here (mirrors the provenance_generator floor). With the default
513	    # threshold=2 it would already fail as overlap_too_low, but this fail-closes the
514	    # PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP=0 edge (where overlap=0 is not < 0) and gives a precise
515	    # reason. Faithfulness-TIGHTENING: any content word OR decimal routes to the numeric/overlap
516	    # floors, so a real clinical sentence can never trip this.
517	    if not sentence_words and not sentence_decimals:
518	        return False, "empty_or_contentless_sentence"
519	    overlap = len(sentence_words & span_words)
520	    if overlap < threshold:
521	        return False, "overlap_too_low"
522	
523	    # Check (f) — entailment judge (I-bug-092). Synthesis claims with no
524	    # tokens already short-circuited above; if a synthesis claim DOES
525	    # carry tokens it must clear the same content-correctness bar as
526	    # any other cited sentence.
527	    mode = _entailment_mode()
528	    if mode in ("warn", "enforce"):
529	        verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)
530	        _record_judge_outcome(verdict, reason)
531	        # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: the judge FAILS OPEN to ("ENTAILED","judge_error:...") on an API/parse
532	        # error. Counting that as a genuine ENTAILED ships an UNVERIFIED clinical claim as "verified" — a
533	        # silent downgrade of the binding faithfulness gate (LAW II / lethal-in-clinical). Detect it and
534	        # FAIL CLOSED in enforce mode (drop the sentence, like an unsupported one); warn-mode logs only.
535	        if isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
536	            # I-deepfix-001 W11-section-verify-judge-error-degrade (#1344): a judge_error is
537	            # a TRANSPORT fault (the judge socket failed / returned unparseable), NOT a
538	            # genuine NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED faithfulness verdict. The mechanical provenance
539	            # gates (a)-(e) ALREADY passed above (evidence-id, span-bounds, decimals, >=2
540	            # content-word overlap), so this is an otherwise span-grounded claim being
541	            # dropped purely because of a socket fault. Under a SUSTAINED judge outage the
542	            # enforce-drop can empty EVERY section -> abort_no_verified_sections despite real
543	            # span-grounded evidence. Per the operator-locked "verifier NEVER holds a report":
544	            # when PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE is ON, DEGRADE to KEEP-with-
545	            # disclosed-label (the per-claim 'entailment_unverified_judge_error' label IS the
546	            # faithfulness signal). This applies ONLY to judge_error (transport) — genuine
547	            # NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED below stay DROPPED (faithfulness NOT relaxed). Default-OFF
548	            # preserves the byte-identical enforce-drop.
549	            logger.warning(
550	                "entailment judge_error (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r — failing closed",
551	                mode, sentence_clean, reason,
552	            )
553	            if mode == "enforce":
554	                _je_release = os.environ.get(
555	                    "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE", "0",
556	                ).strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
557	                if _je_release:
558	                    logger.warning(
559	                        "entailment judge_error (mode=enforce): KEEPING span-grounded "
560	                        "sentence with disclosed label (always-release; transport fault, "
561	                        "not a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict): %r", sentence_clean,
562	                    )
563	                    return True, "entailment_unverified_judge_error"
564	                return False, "entailment_judge_error_fail_closed"
565	        if verdict in ("NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
566	            logger.warning(
567	                "entailment %s (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r",
568	                verdict, mode, sentence_clean, reason,
569	            )
570	            if mode == "enforce":
571	                return False, "entailment_failed"
572	
573	    return True, None
574	
575	
576	def verify_sentence_to_record(
577	    sentence_text: str,
578	    section_id: str,
579	    pool: EvidencePool,
580	    min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
581	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
582	) -> VerifiedSentence:
583	    """Convenience: wrap verify_sentence into a VerifiedSentence record.
584	
585	    Used by the generator orchestrator to build Section.verified_sentences.
586	    """
587	    passed, reason = verify_sentence(
588	        sentence_text,
589	        pool,
590	        min_content_overlap=min_content_overlap,
591	        is_synthesis_claim=is_synthesis_claim,
592	    )
593	    tokens = [t.raw for t in extract_tokens(sentence_text)]
594	    # I-deepfix-001 (Codex e2e gate P1): verify_sentence can return (passed=True, reason=<label>)
595	    # for the judge-error always-release path (span-grounded sentence KEPT with a disclosed
596	    # caveat). The VerifiedSentence schema forbids a non-None drop_reason when verifier_pass=True,
597	    # so a KEPT-with-disclosure caveat MUST ride in release_disclosure, not drop_reason — else the
598	    # construction raises and the intended ship-with-label ABORTS. drop_reason stays the
599	    # DROPPED-only reason (passed is False). Faithfulness untouched: NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED still
600	    # fail (passed=False) and carry drop_reason exactly as before.
601	    kept_disclosure_label = reason if (passed and reason is not None) else None
602	    drop_reason = None if passed else reason
603	    return VerifiedSentence(
604	        section_id=section_id,
605	        sentence_text=sentence_text,
606	        provenance_tokens=tokens,
607	        verifier_pass=passed,
608	        drop_reason=drop_reason,
609	        kept_disclosure_label=kept_disclosure_label,
610	        evaluator_agrees=passed,
611	        is_synthesis_claim=is_synthesis_claim,
612	    )
613	
614	
615	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
616	# Section-level rollup
617	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
618	
619	def section_pass_rate(sentences: list[VerifiedSentence]) -> float:
620	    """Fraction of sentences that passed verify, in [0.0, 1.0].
621	
622	    Empty list returns 0.0 (vacuously below any threshold).
623	    """
624	    if not sentences:
625	        return 0.0
626	    passed = sum(1 for s in sentences if s.verifier_pass)
627	    return passed / len(sentences)
628

1	"""Entailment judge — LLM-as-judge for strict_verify check (f).
2	
3	Originally inlined in `polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify`. Extracted
4	per I-bug-099 so future call sites (I-bug-100 OpenRouterClient routing,
5	I-bug-101 FPR audit harness, etc.) can import the judge + telemetry from
6	a single canonical location.
7	
8	I-bug-102 — off-mode contract (verified by `test_off_mode_does_not_instantiate_judge`):
9	when `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off`, the judge is NEVER instantiated:
10	- `_get_judge()` is not called from any code path
11	- `_EntailmentJudge.__init__` does NOT run
12	- No `httpx.Client` is constructed
13	- No OpenRouter network call is made
14	The class definitions are loaded into the module namespace at import
15	time (Python module evaluation), but no judge object exists. Module-
16	level imports are kept lightweight: `openrouter_client` is imported
17	lazily inside the methods that need it (judge construction, ledger
18	write, budget check), so off-mode pays zero cost beyond reading the
19	class definitions.
20	
21	The judge asks an LLM whether a cited SPAN semantically ENTAILS a
22	SENTENCE's specific claims. It runs AFTER mechanical checks (a)-(e) and
23	catches residual fabrication patterns the audit on 2026-05-09 surfaced:
24	  - mechanistic granularity insertion (M2)
25	  - specificity inflation (C2)
26	  - numbers nearby but not entailed (C1)
27	
28	The judge model is the two-family evaluator (Gemma 4 31B by default),
29	matching the §9.1.1 invariant — different lineage from the generator
30	(DeepSeek). Calls go through OpenRouter using the existing project
31	auth substrate. Family segregation is enforced at construction.
32	
33	Public surface (re-exported via `polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify`
34	for backwards compat with monkeypatch test pattern):
35	  - _EntailmentJudge: synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter call
36	  - _get_judge(): lazy singleton accessor
37	  - _JUDGE_TELEMETRY: process-lifetime gate counters
38	  - get_judge_telemetry(): read-only snapshot
39	  - reset_judge_telemetry(): zero in-place
40	  - _record_judge_outcome(): tick counters from judge result
41	
42	NOT moved here (still in strict_verify.py because tests rebind via
43	monkeypatch.setattr on strict_verify):
44	  - _DEFAULT_MODE
45	  - _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED
46	  - _entailment_mode()
47	"""
48	
49	from __future__ import annotations
50	
51	import concurrent.futures
52	import json
53	import logging
54	import os
55	import random
56	import threading
57	import time
58	
59	# I-bug-100: import openrouter_client as a MODULE reference (not by-value)
60	# so monkeypatch.setattr on its globals (`PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN`,
61	# `_COST_LEDGER_PATH`, etc.) propagates to the cost-accounting code paths
62	# in this module. Direct `from … import _COST_LEDGER_PATH` would bind
63	# the value at import time and tests could not override it without
64	# reloading the module.
65	#
66	# I-bug-102 NOTE: this top-level import IS evaluated even in off-mode.
67	# The "skip clinical_generator import" goal in I-bug-102 is interpreted as
68	# "no JUDGE INSTANTIATION + no NETWORK CALL in off-mode" (verified by
69	# `test_off_mode_does_not_instantiate_judge`), NOT as eliminating the
70	# module-import side effect. Empirically the openrouter_client import
71	# is ~50ms cold; off-mode users pay this once at strict_verify import
72	# time. Eliminating it via per-method lazy-import would complicate the
73	# `except _orc.BudgetExceededError` semantics (exception types must be
74	# resolved at except-clause evaluation, which is a hot-path call).
75	# The test asserts the runtime contract; cold-import cost is accepted.
76	from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc
77	# I-deepfix-001 (KEYSTONE de-storm): round-robin burst-spread of the per-claim judge START host across
78	# the mirror chain (stdlib-only leaf helper -> zero off-mode import cost; shared with credibility_judge_caller
79	# so both bursts spread over the SAME chain). Transport-only; verdict logic + fail-closed sentinel untouched.
80	from src.polaris_graph.llm.judge_burst_spread import (
81	    burst_spread_mode as _judge_burst_spread_mode,
82	    next_burst_start_index as _next_burst_start_index,
83	)
84	# I-deepfix-001 fix I3 (judge stability): a process-local per-(model, normalized-claim, normalized-span)
85	# verdict idempotency cache. The SAME (claim, span) recurs across sections (~178 judge calls/report, many
86	# identical), and every repeat is a fresh OpenRouter call that costs money + adds the provider-pool
87	# pressure that 429s the judge and over-drops grounded claims. Caching the FIRST real verdict and serving
88	# identical later calls from it removes the redundant calls WITHOUT changing any verdict (temperature=0.0
89	# => idempotent input). Fail-CLOSED sentinels are never cached (a transient fault stays retryable). Leaf
90	# helper, stdlib-only => zero off-mode import cost. Default-ON; PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE=0 => byte-identical
91	# pre-I3 path. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (returns the judge's own verdict for that exact input). LAW VI.
92	from src.polaris_graph.llm import judge_verdict_cache as _verdict_cache
93	# I-deepfix-001 (de-storm): shared, env-driven bounded-concurrency cap for the per-claim mirror-chain
94	# side-judge POST. Caps how many entailment/credibility/semantic_conflict provider POSTs are IN FLIGHT
95	# at once so the per-claim burst cannot 429-storm the mirror-chain lead host (the friendli 32x-429 the
96	# 2026-07-04 deepener preflight hit). Stdlib-only leaf helper -> zero off-mode import cost. Transport-
97	# only; verdict logic + fail-closed sentinel untouched. Default-ON; PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=0 =>
98	# byte-identical pre-fix (no-op) path. LAW VI.
99	from src.polaris_graph.llm.judge_concurrency import acquire_judge_slot as _acquire_judge_slot
100	
101	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
102	
103	
104	# I-arch-002 (operator 2026-06-13): default to the sovereign open-weight evaluator GLM-5.1 (was the stale
105	# non-reasoning "google/gemma-4-31b-it" leftover — #1249/#1252; only env PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL had masked it).
106	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"  # I-beatboth-008 (#1285, operator-signed 2026-06-20): mirror upgrade 5.1->5.2
107	_ENTAILMENT_TIMEOUT_S = 30.0
108	# I-arch-006 HANG-J1/J2 (#1262): the entailment JUDGE is the verifier and had NO total deadline — a bare
109	# float `httpx.Client(timeout=30.0)` makes 30s the per-read GAP timeout, which httpx RESETS on every byte.
110	# When OpenRouter/Cloudflare holds the socket ESTABLISHED and trickles keep-alive bytes (rx=tx=0 idle-open),
111	# the 30s timer never fires and a single judge POST runs UNBOUNDED (drb_78 hung the whole run on this; a
112	# 14-min judge call was observed). Fix: an EXPLICIT httpx.Timeout with a TIGHT read-stall (no bytes for
113	# `read` seconds → ReadTimeout → the existing bounded same-provider retry reopens a FRESH socket), so a
114	# genuinely dead socket (the observed rx=tx=0 signature) trips in seconds instead of hanging. httpx closes
115	# the connection on a ReadTimeout, and explicit keepalive-expiry reaps the half-open CLOSE_WAIT sockets the
116	# old pooled client leaked on the error path (HANG-J2). Transport/observability only — the verdict logic,
117	# the fail-CLOSED `judge_error:` sentinel, and the two-family/model lock are all UNCHANGED. (LAW VI: env-driven.)
118	_ENTAILMENT_CONNECT_S = float(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_CONNECT_S", "30"))
119	_ENTAILMENT_READ_STALL_S = float(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_READ_STALL_S", "120"))
120	_ENTAILMENT_WRITE_S = float(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_WRITE_S", "60"))
121	_ENTAILMENT_POOL_S = float(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_POOL_S", "30"))
122	_ENTAILMENT_MAX_KEEPALIVE = int(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_MAX_KEEPALIVE", "8"))
123	_ENTAILMENT_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S = float(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S", "30"))
124	# I-arch-006 HANG-J3 (overnight 2026-06-15): the read-stall (read=_ENTAILMENT_READ_STALL_S) is a per-read
125	# GAP timeout that httpx RESETS on every received byte. OpenRouter/Cloudflare holds the judge socket
126	# ESTABLISHED and TRICKLES keep-alive bytes (SSE comment lines / chunked keep-alives), so the gap timer
127	# never elapses and a SINGLE judge POST runs UNBOUNDED — 15-22 min hangs were observed freezing entire
128	# iarch006 resume runs (the run can't finish its ~2500 per-claim verifies before one call hangs forever).
129	# HANG-J1/J2's read-stall only catches a TRULY dead rx=tx=0 socket; it CANNOT bound a trickle. Fix: a HARD
130	# TOTAL per-call wall-deadline around the blocking POST (below). On timeout the hung socket is force-closed
131	# (unsticks the worker) and the existing bounded SAME-provider retry reopens a FRESH connection; on
132	# exhaustion the UNCHANGED fail-CLOSED ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel fires (consumers DROP ->
133	# faithfulness-safe, never fabricates). Transport-only; verdict logic + the fail-closed contract UNCHANGED.
134	# LAW VI: env-driven. Default 150s comfortably exceeds a real high-effort GLM-5.1 NLI call (~6-40s observed)
135	# while bounding the trickle hang; with the 2 retries the worst-case wall per claim is ~3*150s before drop.
136	_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S = float(os.getenv("PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S", "150"))
137	
138	
139	def _post_with_total_deadline(client, endpoint, headers, json_body, total_s):
140	    """HANG-J3: run the blocking judge POST under a HARD total wall-deadline.
141	
142	    httpx's ``read`` timeout is a per-byte GAP that a trickled keep-alive socket resets indefinitely;
143	    this bounds the WHOLE call so one hung judge POST can never freeze the run. Runs the POST on a
144	    worker thread and waits at most ``total_s``. On timeout the client is force-closed (so the worker's
145	    hung read errors out and the thread exits) and ``concurrent.futures.TimeoutError`` is re-raised for
146	    the caller's bounded retry to reopen a fresh connection. Returns the ``httpx.Response`` on success.
147	
148	    I-deepfix-001 (de-storm): the POST runs under the shared, env-driven bounded side-judge concurrency
149	    cap (``acquire_judge_slot``) so at most ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` provider POSTs are in
150	    flight at once — the per-claim burst can no longer 429-storm the mirror-chain lead host. The slot is
151	    held only for the duration of THIS POST (not the caller's retry backoffs) and is released on every
152	    exit path, so a timed-out call frees its slot when the total-deadline force-closes it. Transport-
153	    only: the verdict logic + the fail-closed sentinel are unchanged.
154	    """
155	    with _acquire_judge_slot():
156	        ex = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
157	        try:
158	            fut = ex.submit(client.post, endpoint, headers=headers, json=json_body)
159	        except RuntimeError as _shutdown_exc:
160	            # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) shutdown-race: a straggler judge retry fired AFTER the run finalized
161	            # and the interpreter began shutting down -> ThreadPoolExecutor.submit raises
162	            # 'cannot schedule new futures after interpreter shutdown'. Per-claim verification is already
163	            # complete, so map this to the existing bounded-retry TimeoutError path: the fail-closed
164	            # ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel fires (consumers DROP -> faithfulness-safe) instead of
165	            # an UNHANDLED RuntimeError reaching the run driver as status=error_unexpected. Transport-only;
166	            # verdict logic + the fail-closed contract UNCHANGED (faithfulness-neutral).
167	            ex.shutdown(wait=False)
168	            raise concurrent.futures.TimeoutError(
169	                "interpreter_shutdown_cannot_schedule_judge_future"
170	            ) from _shutdown_exc
171	        try:
172	            return fut.result(timeout=total_s)
173	        except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
174	            try:
175	                client.close()  # force the hung socket closed -> the worker's blocked read unblocks + exits
176	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
177	                pass
178	            raise
179	        finally:
180	            # Codex P2 (HANG-J3 gate): deterministic executor teardown on EVERY exit path (success,
181	            # timeout, or a non-timeout client.post exception). wait=False so a still-unsticking worker
182	            # never blocks us.
183	            ex.shutdown(wait=False)
184	# I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 4 (LAW VI — no magic numbers): bounded SAME-provider retry count
185	# for a single judge call. A transient transport/parse/empty-choices fault on the entailment judge
186	# previously fell straight through to the fail-open ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel, which the
187	# consumers DROP in enforce mode — an OVER-DROP of a salvageable verdict (the drb_72-class coverage
188	# collapse). Retry the POST+parse this many extra times before emitting the sentinel; default 2
189	# (=> up to 3 total attempts). 0 disables the retry (single attempt, the pre-#1191 behavior).
190	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES = 2
191	# I-arch-007 A2 (entailment-speed fix): a total-deadline (trickle-hang) timeout re-hangs on the SAME
192	# pinned mirror provider, so the extra same-provider retries are ~_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S of dead wait that
193	# almost never recovers — the dominant contributor to the hang-path worst case (3x150s=450s). Cap a
194	# total_deadline_exceeded retry TIGHTER than the general transient-fault budget. DEFAULT here PRESERVES
195	# current behaviour (= the general retry count, so the OFF path is byte-identical, LAW VI); the run slate
196	# sets PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1 to apply the fix (=> up to 2 attempts on a hang vs 3 on a
197	# transient fault). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: the verdict emitted on exhaustion is the SAME fail-closed
198	# ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel the consumers DROP — fewer wasted retries, never a different verdict.
199	_ENV_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES = "PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES"
200	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES
201	# Short fixed backoff between judge retries (seconds). Env-overridable per LAW VI.
202	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRY_BACKOFF_S = 0.5
203	# U16 (I-deepfix-001): rate-limit / connectivity hardening. A real HTTP 429/503/502/504, a DNS
204	# "name resolution" ConnectError, or an OpenRouter 200-wrapped 429 error-envelope previously got the
205	# SAME tiny fixed backoff (0.5s) + tiny retry budget (2) as a bad-verdict, so a transient account-QPS
206	# 429 storm / DNS blip exhausted the ~3 attempts in ~1s and the claim fail-closed DROPPED — over ~178
207	# claims/report x bounded-parallel verify workers this self-sustained the storm, tore the seam, and
208	# blocked drb_75 checkpoint-resume. Fix (transport-only, faithfulness-NEUTRAL): a recoverable rate-

316	def _no_choices_reason(data: object) -> str | None:
317	    """I-wire-008 (#1322): classify a provider-HEALTH transport fault in a 200 body BEFORE the
318	    bare ``data["choices"][0]`` index. Returns a short reason string when the body cannot yield a
319	    verdict (an OpenRouter error envelope returned as a 200, or a missing/empty ``choices`` list),
320	    else ``None``. Treating these as a typed transport fault (vs a bare KeyError) lets the judge
321	    ROTATE to the next healthy host immediately instead of re-POSTing the same flaky provider."""
322	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
323	        return "non_dict_body"
324	    if data.get("error"):
325	        # OpenRouter sometimes returns its error envelope with HTTP 200 (e.g. a 429 payload).
326	        # U16 (I-deepfix-001): when that envelope carries a rate-limit / upstream-unavailable code
327	        # (429/502/503/504), thread a DISTINCT `rate_limit_200` reason so the retry loop treats it as
328	        # a recoverable rate-limit fault (larger budget + floored/backoff) rather than a generic
329	        # error-body. Non-rate-limit error envelopes keep the byte-identical `error_body_200` reason
330	        # (iwire008 #1322 rotation path unchanged).
331	        _err = data.get("error")
332	        _code = _err.get("code") if isinstance(_err, dict) else None
333	        try:
334	            _code_int = int(_code) if _code is not None else None
335	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
336	            _code_int = None
337	        if _code_int in _RATE_LIMIT_HTTP_STATUSES:
338	            return "rate_limit_200"
339	        return "error_body_200"
340	    choices = data.get("choices")
341	    if not choices or not isinstance(choices, list):
342	        return "no_choices"
343	    return None

366	def _is_transport_poison(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
367	    """True iff `exc` indicates a CLOSED/poisoned httpx transport that a fresh client would heal.
368	
369	    Match is on the exception TYPE name + its string form ONLY (the spec is explicit: NOT a parse
370	    fault). A `json.JSONDecodeError`/`KeyError`/bad-verdict is a payload fault → returns False →
371	    retried WITHOUT a rebuild. Extra markers can be supplied via PG_ENTAILMENT_TRANSPORT_POISON_MARKERS
372	    (comma-separated) per LAW VI; never raises.
373	    """
374	    try:
375	        if isinstance(exc, (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError)):
376	            # Payload/parse faults are NOT transport poison — explicit exclusion (ValueError covers
377	            # json.JSONDecodeError's base + the bad-verdict path's string handling). A genuine
378	            # ssl.SSLError is NOT a subclass of these, so this exclusion never swallows a real
379	            # transport poison.
380	            return False
381	        markers = list(_TRANSPORT_POISON_SUBSTRINGS)
382	        extra = os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_TRANSPORT_POISON_MARKERS", "").strip()
383	        if extra:
384	            markers.extend(m.strip().lower() for m in extra.split(",") if m.strip())
385	        haystack = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}".lower()
386	        return any(marker in haystack for marker in markers)
387	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — predicate must never break the retry loop
388	        return False

452	def _extract_retry_after_s(exc: BaseException) -> float | None:
453	    """U16: parse a ``Retry-After`` header off an ``httpx.HTTPStatusError`` response, in seconds.
454	
455	    OpenRouter's 2026 rate-limit guidance names ``Retry-After`` as the PRIMARY delay source on a 429/
456	    503. Supports the two RFC-7231 forms — a non-negative integer number of seconds, or an HTTP-date
457	    (delta from now, clamped at >= 0). Returns ``None`` when the exception is not an HTTP status error,
458	    has no response, or the header is absent/unparseable (the caller then uses the exponential floor).
459	    Never raises."""
460	    try:
461	        import httpx  # local import: off-mode zero cost
462	
463	        if not isinstance(exc, httpx.HTTPStatusError):
464	            return None
465	        resp = getattr(exc, "response", None)
466	        if resp is None:
467	            return None
468	        raw = (resp.headers.get("Retry-After") or "").strip()
469	        if not raw:
470	            return None
471	        try:
472	            return max(0.0, float(int(raw)))
473	        except ValueError:
474	            pass
475	        import datetime as _dt
476	        from email.utils import parsedate_to_datetime
477	
478	        when = parsedate_to_datetime(raw)
479	        if when is None:
480	            return None
481	        if when.tzinfo is None:
482	            when = when.replace(tzinfo=_dt.timezone.utc)
483	        return max(0.0, (when - _dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc)).total_seconds())
484	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — Retry-After parsing must never break the retry loop
485	        return None
486

488	def _rate_limit_backoff_s(
489	    attempt: int,
490	    retry_after: float | None,
491	    floor: float,
492	    cap: float,
493	    base: float,
494	) -> float:
495	    """U16: the delay (seconds) before the next retry of a rate-limit / connectivity fault.
496	
497	    Honor a server ``Retry-After`` VERBATIM when present (clamped to ``cap``). Otherwise use
498	    exponential backoff with FULL jitter bounded to ``[floor, cap]``: the deterministic exponential
499	    ``max(floor, base**(attempt+1)) * (attempt+1)`` (mirrors openrouter_client.py's 429 formula),
500	    capped at ``cap``, then jittered uniformly between ``floor`` and that capped value (AWS
501	    exp-backoff-and-jitter). The result is always ``>= floor`` (a rate-limit window needs a real wait)
502	    and ``<= cap`` (bounded worst-case wall). Deterministic when ``capped_exp == floor``."""
503	    if retry_after is not None:
504	        return max(0.0, min(cap, retry_after))
505	    capped_exp = min(cap, max(floor, base ** (attempt + 1)) * (attempt + 1))
506	    if capped_exp <= floor:
507	        return floor
508	    return random.uniform(floor, capped_exp)

511	def _extract_first_json_object(content: object) -> dict:
512	    """I-arch-011 (B12): parse the FIRST complete JSON object out of a possibly-garbled
513	    judge response and return it as a dict.
514	
515	    Motivating fault: a "garbled-200" = a valid JSON verdict object followed by trailing
516	    reasoning text, e.g. ``{"verdict": "ENTAILED", "reason": "ok"}\\nThe span supports...``.
517	    A bare ``json.loads(content)`` raises ``json.JSONDecodeError: Extra data: line 2 ...``
518	    on that input and the salvageable verdict is thrown away (over-drop, the drb_72-class
519	    coverage collapse).
520	
521	    Implementation: walk every ``{`` index and use ``json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode`` —
522	    the stdlib decoder that parses ONE complete value and ignores trailing data. This is
523	    brace-/quote-/escape-correct (a naive depth counter miscounts braces inside string
524	    values such as ``"dose {x} mg"``). The first ``{`` that decodes to a dict wins, so a
525	    response with prose BEFORE the JSON object is also rescued.
526	
527	    Selection rule: with ``response_format`` removed (B14), a prose-wrapped or multi-object
528	    body is now the normal case, so "first dict wins" could select the WRONG object (e.g.
529	    a leading ``{"note": "..."}`` ahead of the real ``{"verdict": ...}``). To avoid that —
530	    and the fail-closed -> fail-open regression it would cause on the strict consumer — this
531	    returns the first complete object that actually CARRIES the required ``"verdict"`` field,
532	    and RAISES if the scan finds none. The scan advances past each decoded object by its end
533	    offset (not one char) so a nested sub-object is never mis-selected.
534	
535	    Fail-CLOSED contract (faithfulness): this function RAISES (never returns a default /
536	    sentinel / partial) on empty/None/non-str content, on content with no ``{``, on a lone
537	    ``{`` or otherwise malformed/partial JSON, on a first value that is not a JSON object,
538	    AND on a valid object that lacks ``"verdict"``. The caller's existing fail-closed path
539	    (retry then drop / hold) is therefore preserved byte-for-byte for every genuine failure —
540	    only the valid-verdict-object-plus-trailing-text case is newly rescued.
541	    """
542	    if not isinstance(content, str) or not content.strip():
543	        raise ValueError("empty or non-str judge content")
544	    decoder = json.JSONDecoder()
545	    search_from = 0
546	    while True:
547	        # Scan for the next TOP-LEVEL JSON value, taking the EARLIEST of the next "{" or "[" so
548	        # we raw_decode the OUTERMOST value and NEVER descend into a container's interior.
549	        obj_idx = content.find("{", search_from)
550	        arr_idx = content.find("[", search_from)
551	        candidates = [i for i in (obj_idx, arr_idx) if i != -1]
552	        if not candidates:
553	            raise ValueError("no verdict-bearing JSON object found in judge content")
554	        start = min(candidates)
555	        try:
556	            value, end = decoder.raw_decode(content, start)
557	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
558	            # FAIL-CLOSED (Codex P1): a "{"/"[" that does NOT begin a COMPLETE JSON value means
559	            # malformed / partial / truncated content. Do NOT advance into its interior — that
560	            # would salvage a nested verdict out of a malformed envelope (e.g.
561	            # '{"wrapper":{"verdict":"NEUTRAL"}') and turn fail-closed into fail-open. Raise so
562	            # the caller's existing retry/drop/hold path fires.
563	            raise ValueError(
564	                f"malformed JSON in judge content at offset {start}: {exc}"
565	            ) from exc
566	        if isinstance(value, dict) and "verdict" in value:
567	            return value
568	        # A COMPLETE value that is NOT a verdict object — a non-verdict object, an ARRAY (a
569	        # verdict nested in [ ... ] must FAIL CLOSED, not be pulled out — Codex iter-3 P1), or a
570	        # scalar — is skipped PAST by its end offset; we keep scanning at the TOP LEVEL and never
571	        # descend inside it. A verdict buried in a container is treated as absent (fail-closed).
572	        search_from = end
573

631	class _EntailmentJudge:
632	    """Synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter entailment call.
633

635	    PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off (the default before I-bug-095).
636	    """
637	
638	    def __init__(self) -> None:
639	        import httpx  # local import: avoid forcing the dep when off
640	
641	        # Lazy-import the family-segregation check so the off-mode path
642	        # never touches openrouter_client. The judge is acting as a
643	        # content evaluator (Layer-2), so it MUST differ from the
644	        # generator family per CLAUDE.md §9.1.1 — fail at construction
645	        # if PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL is in the same family as PG_GENERATOR_MODEL.
646	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import check_family_segregation
647	
648	        api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
649	        if not api_key:
650	            raise RuntimeError(
651	                "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
652	            )
653	        self._api_key = api_key
654	        # I-sov-001: endpoint is env-configurable so the sovereign deploy
655	        # can point the entailment judge at the OVH H200 vLLM endpoint
656	        # (set OPENROUTER_BASE_URL=http://<priv-ip>:8000/v1). Default keeps
657	        # OpenRouter. Mirrors openrouter_client.py:43-45.
658	        base_url = os.environ.get(
659	            "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
660	        ).rstrip("/")
661	        self._endpoint = f"{base_url}/chat/completions"
662	        self._model = os.environ.get(
663	            "PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL
664	        )
665	        # Two-family invariant per §9.1.1: raises RuntimeError if the
666	        # entailment judge ends up in the same family as the generator
667	        # (e.g. an operator setting PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL to a DeepSeek
668	        # variant when PG_GENERATOR_MODEL is also DeepSeek). The
669	        # default model (google/gemma-4-31b-it) is in a different
670	        # family from DeepSeek by construction.
671	        check_family_segregation(evaluator_model=self._model)
672	        # I-arch-007 ITEM 2a (thread-safety): the judge is a process-lifetime SINGLETON (_get_judge)
673	        # shared across the ThreadPoolExecutor verifier workers. A SHARED mutable httpx client that one
674	        # worker force-closes (the HANG-J3 total-deadline path) while a sibling is mid-`post` is exactly
675	        # the cross-thread close-while-in-flight race that produced Q78's `[X509] PEM lib` TLS-state
676	        # corruption (httpx Discussion #1633 / httpcore #550 explicitly discourage it). Fix: give each
677	        # worker thread its OWN client via threading.local(); the force-close/rebuild in
678	        # _post_with_total_deadline then only ever touches the calling thread's client. The `_tls` store
679	        # MUST exist before the first `self._client` access below (the property reads it).
680	        self._tls = threading.local()
681	        # I-arch-006 HANG-J1/J2 (#1262) + HANG-J3 (2026-06-15): build via _build_client so the HANG-J3
682	        # total-deadline path can force-close + REBUILD a fresh client after a trickle hang.
683	        self._client = self._build_client()
684	
685	    @property
686	    def _client(self):
687	        """The current thread's httpx judge client (I-arch-007 ITEM 2a — thread-local).
688	
689	        Lazily builds a per-thread client on first read (each verifier worker gets its OWN client,
690	        so a sibling's force-close can never poison this thread's transport) and HEALS a closed one
691	        on the next read. The `is_closed is True` guard is deliberate: a real httpx client exposes a
692	        BOOL `is_closed`, but injected test doubles do not — a `MagicMock().is_closed` is a truthy
693	        child mock (NOT `is True`) and a `_FakeJudgeClient` has no such attr (defaults False via
694	        getattr), so neither spuriously triggers a rebuild that would replace the injected stub. This
695	        keeps every existing `judge._client = <stub>` test contract byte-intact while only a genuinely
696	        CLOSED real client self-heals.
697	        """
698	        client = getattr(self._tls, "client", None)
699	        if client is None or getattr(client, "is_closed", False) is True:
700	            client = self._build_client()
701	            self._tls.client = client
702	        return client
703	
704	    @_client.setter
705	    def _client(self, value) -> None:
706	        # Preserve the existing test-injection contract (`judge._client = <stub/MagicMock>`): writes go
707	        # to THIS thread's thread-local slot, and a subsequent read returns the same object. Production
708	        # rebuild sites (`self._client = self._build_client()`) likewise rebind only the calling thread's
709	        # client — the thread-safety guarantee.
710	        self._tls.client = value
711	
712	    def _build_client(self):
713	        """Construct the entailment httpx client. HANG-J1/J2: explicit per-phase read-stall (a dead
714	        rx=tx=0 socket trips fast and the retry reopens) + bounded keepalive so half-open CLOSE_WAIT
715	        sockets are reaped. The HANG-J3 total-deadline (see judge()) bounds a TRICKLED socket the
716	        read-stall alone cannot. Verdict logic untouched."""
717	        import httpx  # local import: avoid forcing the dep when off
718	
719	        # BUG 3 (X509 SSL race): pass the process-wide shared, cert-verifying
720	        # ssl.SSLContext so httpx does NOT re-parse the PEM bundle on this
721	        # per-thread client build. The Q78 run-killer was concurrent thread-local
722	        # entailment client construction racing ssl.create_default_context's
723	        # PEM parse -> `[X509] PEM lib`. TLS verification stays ENABLED — the
724	        # shared context is CERT_REQUIRED + check_hostname (verify-neutral).
725	        from src.utils.shared_ssl_context import get_shared_ssl_context
726	        return httpx.Client(
727	            verify=get_shared_ssl_context(),
728	            timeout=httpx.Timeout(
729	                connect=_ENTAILMENT_CONNECT_S,
730	                read=_ENTAILMENT_READ_STALL_S,
731	                write=_ENTAILMENT_WRITE_S,
732	                pool=_ENTAILMENT_POOL_S,
733	            ),
734	            limits=httpx.Limits(
735	                max_keepalive_connections=_ENTAILMENT_MAX_KEEPALIVE,
736	                keepalive_expiry=_ENTAILMENT_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S,
737	            ),
738	        )
739	
740	    def judge(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
741	        """Return (verdict, reason) — the public entry, with the I3 verdict idempotency cache.
742	
743	        I-deepfix-001 fix I3: a process-local cache keyed on (model, normalized sentence, normalized
744	        span). On a hit the cached (verdict, reason) is returned WITHOUT a network call — the SAME tuple
745	        the judge already produced for this exact input (temperature=0.0 => idempotent), so every
746	        downstream strict_verify / provenance decision + telemetry is byte-identical. On a miss the real
747	        judge (`_judge_uncached`) runs and its verdict is cached — EXCEPT the fail-CLOSED
748	        ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel, which is never cached so a transient fault stays retryable.
749	        Default-ON; ``PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE=0`` makes this wrapper a pass-through (byte-identical pre-I3).
750	        Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: the cache can only echo a verdict the judge itself returned; it can never
751	        manufacture, relax, or flip one. The two-family/model lock + fail-closed contract are untouched."""
752	        # I3 P1 (Fable gate iter1): the verdict is a function of the RESOLVED entailment prompt, which is a
753	        # call-time variable (PG_ENTAILMENT_PROMPT_VARIANT). Include the active variant in the cache key so
754	        # a same-process bakeoff can never be served one variant's verdict for another variant's prompt.
755	        variant = _active_prompt_variant()
756	        cached = _verdict_cache.get(self._model, sentence, span, variant)
757	        if cached is not None:
758	            return cached
759	        verdict, reason = self._judge_uncached(sentence, span)
760	        # `put` refuses the fail-closed sentinel itself (defensive), so a transient judge_error is never
761	        # memoized — a later identical call re-issues a fresh attempt at a real verdict.
762	        _verdict_cache.put(self._model, sentence, span, verdict, reason, variant)
763	        return verdict, reason
764	
765	    def _judge_uncached(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
766	        """Return (verdict, reason) — the UNCACHED judge call (the I3 cache wraps this in `judge`).
767	
768	        verdict is one of "ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED".
769	
770	        I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 4: a transient transport/parse/empty-choices/bad-verdict
771	        fault is now RETRIED on the same provider up to `PG_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES` extra times
772	        (default 2 => 3 total attempts; env-driven per LAW VI) before the method emits the
773	        ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...') sentinel on exhaustion. This is NOT a fail-OPEN: both
774	        consumers FAIL CLOSED on that sentinel — `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:295-301`
775	        keys on the `judge_error:` PREFIX alone (drops in enforce); `provenance_generator.py:1795`
776	        sets `judge_error_flag` on `verdict=='ENTAILED' AND reason.startswith('judge_error:')`
777	        (consumed -> drop). The sentinel stays EXACTLY ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...') so that
778	        detection is preserved (do NOT flip to NEUTRAL standalone — that bypasses the
779	        provenance detection at :1795). The retry's only effect is to stop a SINGLE transient
780	        judge fault from OVER-DROPPING a salvageable verdict (the drb_72-class coverage collapse),
781	        without loosening the binding faithfulness gate.
782	
783	        I-bug-100: after each successful httpx call this method records
784	        the call cost via openrouter_client's module-level helpers
785	        (`_add_run_cost`, `check_run_budget`, `_COST_LEDGER_PATH`) so
786	        judge spend is visible to the per-run budget cap and the cost
787	        ledger. `BudgetExceededError` is explicitly re-raised before
788	        the broad retry/fail-closed handler so a cap breach aborts the
789	        sweep cleanly instead of being masked as a transient judge error
790	        or being retried.
791	        """
792	        prompt = _select_entailment_prompt().format(span=span, sentence=sentence)
793	        started = time.monotonic()
794	        # I-bug-946 (#932): when Path-B gate is active, force singleton provider routing in
795	        # the request body to match the resolved-at-preflight per-role provider. Without this,
796	        # this direct httpx path bypasses the gate's routing intent (the OpenRouterClient path
797	        # also got the override via openrouter_client.py:1400-1410). Codex iter-2 P1#2.
798	        # Codex iter-1 diff P1#2: this lookup MUST use an explicit role string, NOT the ambient
799	        # _ROLE contextvar. The entailment judge fires during section generation (where
800	        # _ROLE=="generator"), but it posts the evaluator-family model — using the ambient role
801	        # would route the judge model to the generator's provider.
802	        # I-arch-004 F09: route via "mirror" (the LOCKED 4-role key), NOT the RETIRED "evaluator"
803	        # key. The preflight-resolved role_provider_map only carries generator/mirror/sentinel/judge
804	        # (pathB_runner._LOCKED_ROLES); the legacy "evaluator" key is absent, so get_role_provider(
805	        # "evaluator") returned None -> NO provider pin -> this side-judge FREE-ROUTED to an unpinned
806	        # provider instead of the locked mirror chain. Per polaris_runtime_lock.yaml:legacy_compat
807	        # the retired evaluator role maps_to_role: mirror (GLM-5.1), so the side-judge pins to the
808	        # SAME provider chain as the main mirror role (allow_fallbacks=False, require_parameters=True).
809	        try:
810	            from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_for_routing
811	            _gate_provider = _pathb_for_routing.get_role_provider("mirror")
812	        except Exception:
813	            _gate_provider = None
814	        # Operator 2026-06-13: reasoning stays MAX; any sub-max/off effort is coerced UP to high so the
815	        # NLI verdict is never starved. Un-starved max_tokens lets the high-effort reasoning complete AND
816	        # emit the JSON verdict (the old max_tokens=100 truncated mid-reasoning -> empty -> coverage collapse).
817	        _ent_effort = (os.environ.get(_ENV_ENTAILMENT_REASONING_EFFORT, "").strip().lower()
818	                       or _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_REASONING_EFFORT)
819	        if _ent_effort not in ("high", "xhigh"):
820	            _ent_effort = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_REASONING_EFFORT
821	        try:
822	            _ent_maxtok = max(256, int(os.environ.get(_ENV_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS)
823	                                       or _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS))

1	"""Provenance token format + parser for slice 003 generator output.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_003/architecture_proposal.md` §"provenance.py" and
4	CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 2 ('every generated sentence carries
5	[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>] tokens').
6	
7	Token format: `[#ev:<source_id>:<start>-<end>]` where:
8	- source_id is a UUID-shaped string (lowercase hex with optional dashes)
9	  matching the source_id field of `polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool.Source`
10	- start, end are non-negative integer character offsets into the source's
11	  full_text (or snippet, when full_text is unavailable)
12	- start <= end is enforced; start == end is allowed for empty-span citations
13	  (rare but valid for short citations like punctuation marks)
14	
15	Pure-functions module: no I/O, no LLM, no network. Suitable for fast tests.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import re
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	
23	from src.polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool, Source
24	
25	
26	# Source.source_id defaults to uuid.uuid4() (lowercase hex + dashes) but is
27	# typed as `str`, so callers may supply any reasonable identifier (e.g.
28	# 'src-1', 'pubmed_12345'). Token regex accepts the broader alphanumeric
29	# + dash + underscore form. Bounded to 100 chars so a runaway token can't
30	# DoS the parser.
31	PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(
32	    r"\[#ev:([A-Za-z0-9_][A-Za-z0-9_\-]{0,99}):(\d+)-(\d+)\]"
33	)
34	
35	
36	@dataclass(frozen=True)
37	class ProvenanceToken:
38	    """A parsed `[#ev:src:s-e]` token with bounds extracted as ints."""
39	
40	    source_id: str
41	    span_start: int
42	    span_end: int
43	    raw: str
44	
45	    def __str__(self) -> str:
46	        return self.raw
47	
48	
49	def extract_tokens(sentence: str) -> list[ProvenanceToken]:
50	    """Find every well-formed provenance token in `sentence`.
51	
52	    Returns parsed ProvenanceToken instances in left-to-right order. A
53	    sentence with no tokens returns []. Malformed candidates that LOOK
54	    like tokens but don't match the regex (e.g. uppercase hex,
55	    `[#ev:abc:bad-bounds]`) are silently skipped — strict_verify is the
56	    layer that rejects sentences for missing/invalid tokens.
57	    """
58	    out: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
59	    for match in PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.finditer(sentence):
60	        try:
61	            start = int(match.group(2))
62	            end = int(match.group(3))
63	        except ValueError:
64	            continue
65	        out.append(
66	            ProvenanceToken(
67	                source_id=match.group(1),
68	                span_start=start,
69	                span_end=end,
70	                raw=match.group(0),
71	            )
72	        )
73	    return out
74	
75	
76	def strip_tokens(sentence: str) -> str:
77	    """Return `sentence` with every well-formed token removed.
78	
79	    Whitespace around removed tokens is collapsed; leading/trailing
80	    whitespace is stripped. Useful for comparing the human-prose part of
81	    a sentence against the cited span (per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3-4).
82	    """
83	    stripped = PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence)
84	    # Collapse internal runs of whitespace introduced by token removal
85	    stripped = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped)
86	    return stripped.strip()
87	
88	
89	def has_any_token(sentence: str) -> bool:
90	    """True iff at least one well-formed provenance token is present."""
91	    return PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.search(sentence) is not None
92	
93	
94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	# Validation against EvidencePool
96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	
98	class TokenValidationError(str):
99	    """Reason a token failed validation against the pool. Plain string
100	    enum; used in strict_verify drop_reason mapping."""
101	
102	    UNKNOWN_SOURCE_ID = "invalid_token"
103	    SPAN_OUT_OF_RANGE = "span_out_of_range"
104	
105	
106	def _source_text_length(source: Source) -> int:
107	    """Return the length of the text the token is allowed to reference.
108	
109	    Prefers full_text when available; falls back to snippet length.
110	    """
111	    if source.full_text is not None:
112	        return len(source.full_text)
113	    return len(source.snippet)
114	
115	
116	def validate_token_against_pool(
117	    token: ProvenanceToken,
118	    pool: EvidencePool,
119	) -> str | None:
120	    """Validate a single token against the pool.
121	
122	    Returns:
123	        None — token is valid (CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariants 1+2 met)
124	        TokenValidationError.UNKNOWN_SOURCE_ID — source_id not in pool
125	        TokenValidationError.SPAN_OUT_OF_RANGE — span bounds invalid
126	    """
127	    source = _find_source(pool, token.source_id)
128	    if source is None:
129	        return TokenValidationError.UNKNOWN_SOURCE_ID
130	
131	    if token.span_start < 0:
132	        return TokenValidationError.SPAN_OUT_OF_RANGE
133	    if token.span_start > token.span_end:
134	        return TokenValidationError.SPAN_OUT_OF_RANGE
135	
136	    text_len = _source_text_length(source)
137	    if token.span_end > text_len:
138	        return TokenValidationError.SPAN_OUT_OF_RANGE
139	
140	    return None
141	
142	
143	def _find_source(pool: EvidencePool, source_id: str) -> Source | None:
144	    for source in pool.sources:
145	        if source.source_id == source_id:
146	            return source
147	    return None
148	
149	
150	def get_span_text(token: ProvenanceToken, pool: EvidencePool) -> str | None:
151	    """Return the text of the cited span, or None if token is invalid.
152	
153	    Used by strict_verify to compare sentence content against span content.
154	    Prefers source.full_text; falls back to source.snippet.
155	    """
156	    source = _find_source(pool, token.source_id)
157	    if source is None:
158	        return None
159	    text = source.full_text if source.full_text is not None else source.snippet
160	    if token.span_start < 0 or token.span_end > len(text):
161	        return None
162	    return text[token.span_start : token.span_end]
163
