3598	def strict_verify(
3599	    draft_text: str,
3600	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
3601	    *,
3602	    require_number_match: bool = True,
3603	    telemetry_block: str | None = None,
3604	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
3605	) -> StrictVerificationReport:
3606	    """Run strict verification on a draft. Drops failing sentences.
3607	
3608	    Gap-3 (original): the Limitations paragraph was pass-through because
3609	    it discusses pipeline telemetry, not evidence.
3610	
3611	    BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): when `telemetry_block` is supplied,
3612	    Limitations sentences ARE verified against it — every numeric
3613	    claim in the sentence must appear verbatim in the telemetry block.
3614	    This catches a fabricated "only 3% of sources are T1" claim when
3615	    the telemetry actually says "T1: 9%". Backward-compatible: if
3616	    telemetry_block is None (default), falls back to pass-through.
3617	    """
3618	    findings_text, limitations_text = split_findings_and_limitations(draft_text)
3619	
3620	    kept: list[SentenceVerification] = []
3621	    dropped: list[SentenceVerification] = []
3622	
3623	    # I-pipe-015 (#1240) + I-pipe-016 (#1241) flags, read once per call.
3624	    _honest_tokens = _token_honest_drop_enabled()
3625	    _skip_empty = _skip_empty_enabled()
3626	
3627	    # I-pipe-016 (#1241): content-empty fragments are excluded from total_in
3628	    # (denominator) — they are neither kept nor dropped, they do not count.
3629	    _excluded_empty = 0
3630	
3631	    # BUG-19 (I-arch-006, #1262): pre-gate web-crawl-chrome hygiene. Lazy-load the
3632	    # allowlist-only helpers and strip confirmed crawl-marker LINES from the section
3633	    # text BEFORE sentence-splitting, so a chrome line can't even become a candidate
3634	    # "sentence" (the NTSB 404 "Page not found" hole, where boilerplate trivially
3635	    # self-entails and is COUNTED as verified). `_is_boilerplate` then excludes any
3636	    # surviving pure-chrome / error-page / pure-metadata unit from the gate's INPUT
3637	    # below. INPUT hygiene only — no verdict logic / threshold / gate strictness
3638	    # changes; every pattern is allowlist-only + whole-unit anchored so a real
3639	    # clinical sentence (any language) is never touched. OFF-path (flag=0) is
3640	    # byte-identical: helpers are never loaded, text is untouched.
3641	    _pregate_boilerplate = _pregate_boilerplate_filter_enabled()
3642	    _is_boilerplate = None
3643	    if _pregate_boilerplate:
3644	        _strip_web_boilerplate, _is_boilerplate = _load_boilerplate_helpers()
3645	        findings_text = _strip_web_boilerplate(findings_text)
3646	        limitations_text = _strip_web_boilerplate(limitations_text)
3647	
3648	    # Findings: strict provenance verification
3649	    findings_sentences = split_into_sentences(findings_text)
3650	    # fix#19 (#1262): SEQUENTIAL pre-filter (cheap, deterministic) builds the
3651	    # ORDERED list of canonicalized sentences that need the (expensive) per-sentence
3652	    # judge. It owns _excluded_empty so the denominator semantics are unchanged —
3653	    # only the per-sentence verify-or-rescue call (the blocking entailment judge) is
3654	    # eligible to run in parallel below.
3655	    _findings_to_verify: list[str] = []
3656	    for s in findings_sentences:
3657	        # I-pipe-015 (#1240): canonicalize a malformed `[ev:...]` token to
3658	        # `[#ev:...]` so an otherwise-valid citation is not silently lost. The
3659	        # canonical form is what flows to verify_sentence_provenance (and is
3660	        # stored on .sentence), so downstream token-stripping in
3661	        # resolve_provenance_to_citations works and no malformed literal leaks
3662	        # into the rendered report. The canonicalized token STILL runs the full
3663	        # validation — bracket format is fixed, no check is bypassed.
3664	        if _honest_tokens:
3665	            s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
3666	        # I-pipe-016 (#1241): a content-empty fragment (orphaned citation /
3667	        # punctuation residue with no content word and no number) is excluded
3668	        # from BOTH numerator and denominator. verify_sentence_provenance would
3669	        # already DROP it (BUG-03 / no_provenance_token), so excluding it from
3670	        # the denominator instead of dropping it only removes empty noise from
3671	        # the telemetry; it never lets an unverified REAL sentence through.
3672	        if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
3673	            _excluded_empty += 1
3674	            continue
3675	        # BUG-19 (#1262): a pure web-crawl-chrome / error-page-stub / bare-DOI /
3676	        # table-number "sentence" carries NO assertional claim — it must NOT reach
3677	        # the gate, where boilerplate would trivially self-entail and be COUNTED as
3678	        # verified (the NTSB 404 "Page not found" hole). Exclude it from BOTH
3679	        # numerator and denominator (same faithfulness-neutral treatment as the
3680	        # content-empty path). INPUT hygiene only: the helper is allowlist-only +
3681	        # whole-unit anchored, so a real clinical sentence is never flagged.
3682	        if _is_boilerplate is not None and _is_boilerplate(s):
3683	            _excluded_empty += 1
3684	            continue
3685	        _findings_to_verify.append(s)
3686	
3687	    # fix#19 (#1262): verify each surviving sentence — the SAME verify-or-rescue
3688	    # decision as before, just dispatched either serially (default) or across a
3689	    # BOUNDED thread pool. Verdicts are independent; results are reassembled in the
3690	    # ORIGINAL order so kept/dropped are byte-identical to the serial path.
3691	    _reanchor_enabled = _provenance_reanchor_enabled()
3692	    _verify_workers = _parallel_verify_workers()
3693	    if _verify_workers < 2 or len(_findings_to_verify) < 2:
3694	        # Serial path (default / single-item): byte-identical to the legacy loop.
3695	        _findings_results = [
3696	            _verify_one_findings_sentence(
3697	                s, evidence_pool,
3698	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3699	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3700	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3701	            )
3702	            for s in _findings_to_verify
3703	        ]
3704	    else:
3705	        # Parallel path: a BOUNDED ThreadPoolExecutor caps concurrency at exactly
3706	        # _verify_workers. A fresh worker thread starts with an EMPTY contextvars
3707	        # context, which would silently drop the per-run judge telemetry (FX-09
3708	        # ContextVar holding the run's {calls, judge_error} dict), the ambient role,
3709	        # and the gate's provider pin. So we capture the PARENT context ONCE here in
3710	        # the calling thread and run each task inside a copy of it — the ContextVar
3711	        # still references the SAME run-telemetry dict object, so the in-place
3712	        # ``_run_tel[...] += 1`` ticks land in the right counter. ``map`` preserves
3713	        # input order => the reassembled list is index-aligned with
3714	        # _findings_to_verify (deterministic kept/dropped, never race-ordered). A
3715	        # worker exception PROPAGATES out of map() (fail-loud — a hard gate /
3716	        # programming defect is never swallowed), exactly as the serial loop raises.
3717	        import concurrent.futures as _futures  # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy: zero OFF-path cost)
3718	        import contextvars as _ctxvars  # noqa: PLC0415
3719	
3720	        # I-arch-011 (Codex FIX-C P1): each worker runs in a COPIED context, so the entailment
3721	        # judge's ``_orc._add_run_cost`` / ``check_run_budget`` mutate the COPY's ``_RUN_COST_CTX``
3722	        # (lost to the parent) — the parallel verify spend would bypass PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN and the
3723	        # run-budget gate. The cost LEDGER stays accurate (``append_cost_ledger_row`` bumps a
3724	        # process-global, lock-protected per-session accumulator, NOT a contextvar). Mirror the
3725	        # credibility_pass offload reconciliation (openrouter_client.ledger_cumulative docstring):
3726	        # snapshot the per-session ledger cumulative before/after the pool and re-add the delta to the
3727	        # PARENT ``_RUN_COST_CTX``, then re-check the budget so the gate is inclusive of the parallel
3728	        # judge spend. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (cost accounting only; verdicts unchanged).
3729	        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc_cost  # noqa: PLC0415
3730	        _run_id = _orc_cost._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get()
3731	        _cost_before = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id)
3732	
3733	        _parent_ctx = _ctxvars.copy_context()
3734	
3735	        def _verify_in_context(_s: str) -> SentenceVerification:
3736	            return _parent_ctx.copy().run(
3737	                _verify_one_findings_sentence,
3738	                _s, evidence_pool,
3739	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3740	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3741	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3742	            )
3743	
3744	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_verify_workers) as _pool:
3745	            _findings_results = list(_pool.map(_verify_in_context, _findings_to_verify))
3746	
3747	        # Reconcile the parallel verify spend into the parent budget gate (see note above).
3748	        _cost_delta = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id) - _cost_before
3749	        if _cost_delta > 0:
3750	            _orc_cost._add_run_cost(_cost_delta)
3751	            _orc_cost.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the pool breached the cap
3752	
3753	    for v in _findings_results:
3754	        # Keep iff verified (a re-anchored rescue is returned already is_verified=
3755	        # True), drop otherwise — identical decision to the legacy inline branch.
3756	        if v.is_verified:
3757	            kept.append(v)
3758	        else:
3759	            dropped.append(v)
3760	
3761	    # Limitations: telemetry-grounded verification if block supplied,
3762	    # else pass-through (M-204 backward-compat).
3763	    limitations_sentences = split_into_sentences(limitations_text)
3764	    for s in limitations_sentences:
3765	        # I-pipe-015 (#1240): same malformed-token canonicalization as findings.
3766	        if _honest_tokens:
3767	            s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
3768	        # I-pipe-016 (#1241): exclude content-empty pass-through fragments from
3769	        # the verified numerator AND total_in. This is the PRIMARY inflation
3770	        # site the forensic flagged: the pass-through branch below counted a
3771	        # bare `[#ev:...]` orphan as is_verified=True. A REAL limitations
3772	        # sentence carries telemetry numbers / words and is never excluded.
3773	        if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
3774	            _excluded_empty += 1
3775	            continue
3776	        # BUG-19 (#1262): exclude pure web-crawl-chrome / error-page / pure-metadata
3777	        # units from the limitations branch too — its pass-through path (below)

1	"""I-deepfix-001 B13 (#1357) — bring the Analyst-Synthesis layer UNDER the faithfulness engine.
2	
3	THE PROBLEM (forensic): ``analyst_synthesis.generate_analyst_synthesis`` is architecturally OUTSIDE
4	the faithfulness engine — its system prompt FORBIDS ``[#ev:]`` span tokens and cites by ``[N]``
5	bibliography markers only, so the per-sentence ``strict_verify`` entailment gate NEVER sees it. It is
6	the longest, most confident block in the report, and its grounded over-assertions (hedges upgraded to
7	assertions, interpolated specifics, model-asserted author names absent from the pool) ship UNGATED.
8	
9	THE FIX (the operator-locked "verify AFTER compose = LABEL, never DELETE" pattern, §-1.3 BASKET
10	FAITHFULNESS / `feedback_always_release_verifier_labels_never_holds_2026_06_14`): this module is a
11	thin, additive DEVIATION CHECK over the already-composed synthesis prose. It:
12	
13	  1. splits the synthesis into sentences (reusing ``provenance_generator.split_into_sentences``),
14	  2. for each sentence, parses its ``[N]`` markers (``report_claim_extractor._NUMBERED_RE``) and
15	     resolves ``N -> bibliography[N-1].evidence_id -> the cited evidence row's span``,
16	  3. asks a groundedness judge (the certified MiniMax-M2 Sentinel decomposition by default — a SECOND
17	     family vs the GLM/DeepSeek writer, so two-family holds) whether the cited span SUPPORTS the
18	     sentence,
19	  4. for an UNSUPPORTED sentence appends an inline confidence marker
20	     (``claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(BUCKET_LOW)``), and for a sentence whose ``[N]`` resolve
21	     NO span (``BUCKET_NO_SOURCE``) — KEEP the sentence, NEVER delete it.
22	
23	FAIL-CLOSED to ``BUCKET_LOW``: a judge error / transport flap / timeout LABELS the sentence low (over-
24	labeling is the SAFE direction for a previously-ungated layer), never a silent high, never a hold.
25	
26	FAITHFULNESS ENGINE UNTOUCHED: ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are
27	NOT modified. This is an additive, LABEL-only deviation leg over the previously-ungated synthesis
28	prose. The judge here is ADVISORY — it never drops a sentence, never changes ``is_verified``.
29	
30	LAW VI: the leg is gated default-ON by ``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK`` and shares the coarse
31	``PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS`` kill-switch (when the whole analyst layer is off, this never runs).
32	
33	TESTABILITY / NO HANG (I-arch-006/007 lesson): the per-sentence groundedness call is INJECTABLE
34	(``judge_fn``) so a test passes a deterministic fake; the real path resolves a bounded, deadline-wrapped
35	Sentinel judge lazily. The calls run BOUNDED-PARALLEL (never a serial unbounded loop on a trickle
36	socket — the documented hang class).
37	"""
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import logging
41	import os
42	import re
43	import time
44	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, TimeoutError as _FutureTimeout
45	from typing import Any, Callable

51	)

53	
54	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
55	
56	# LAW VI flags. The leg is default-ON; the coarse analyst-layer kill-switch ALSO gates it (when the
57	# whole analyst layer is off, this module never runs because the caller returns before invoking it).
58	_ENV_ENABLED = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK"
59	_ENV_ANALYST_KILL = "PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS"
60	# Bounded-parallel fan-out for the per-sentence groundedness calls (NEVER a serial unbounded loop on a
61	# trickle socket — the I-arch-006/007 hang class). LAW VI; default 8.
62	_ENV_MAX_INFLIGHT = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_MAX_INFLIGHT"
63	_DEFAULT_MAX_INFLIGHT = 8
64	# Per-call deadline (seconds). A judge socket flap fail-CLOSES to BUCKET_LOW (over-label = safe) rather
65	# than hanging the report. LAW VI; default 60s.
66	_ENV_DEADLINE_S = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_DEADLINE_S"
67	_DEFAULT_DEADLINE_S = 60.0
68	
69	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no", "disabled"})
70	
71	# I-deepfix-001 D3 (#1344) — FAIL-CLOSED PROMOTE gate. Default-OFF (LAW VI). This is the gate that lets
72	# the analyst-synthesis layer turn ON safely (the layer is REQUIRED_OFF today precisely because it was
73	# not span-verified). When this flag is ON:
74	#   * a synthesis sentence renders in the SCORED BODY only if it passes ALL THREE legs of the frozen

80	#       (2) the deterministic span-grounding pre-check (strict_verify §9.1.3 principle — every decimal
81	#           in the sentence appears in the cited span AND >= _MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP shared content words); and
82	#       (3) the D8/NLI groundedness judge (judge_fn / the certified Sentinel).
83	#     A passing sentence is KEEP-and-PROMOTE'd (BUCKET_MODERATE "verified against the cited source");
84	#   * a sentence that FAILS either leg — ungrounded, no-source, or judge-fault — is DROPPED from the
85	#     body (fail-closed) and surfaced in the returned drop telemetry + a fail-LOUD log line, NEVER
86	#     rendered as a hedged body claim. Over-drop is the safe direction; no source is deleted (the drop
87	#     removes a model-generated sentence, not an evidence row), strict_verify / NLI / 4-role are NOT
88	#     touched — this gate is ADDED on top of the frozen engine.
89	# OFF (default) => the legacy advisory KEEP-and-LABEL leg runs, byte-identical: grounded sentences pass
90	# bare, ungrounded/no-source sentences are LABELED (never dropped).
91	_ENV_PROMOTE_GROUNDED = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED"

93	# Deterministic span-grounding leg (the strict_verify §9.1.3 principle, reused faithfully at the
94	# synthesis seam so the D3 gate is the FROZEN ENGINE, not a lone judge). PURE, no I/O.
95	_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP = 2
96	_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^\]]*\]")          # [N] citation + [confidence:...] markers
97	# I-deepfix-001 D3 P1 (#1344): a pre-labeled sentence (already carrying a [confidence:...] marker —
98	# model-injected, or a defensive double-call) must be STRIPPED of that marker so the promote gate
99	# re-screens the bare prose through BOTH frozen-engine legs. Without this a hedged, ungrounded claim
100	# could carry its own [confidence: low] tag past the gate and survive in the scored body.
101	_CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\s*\[confidence:[^\]]*\]")
102	_DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?")
103	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
104	# A tiny stoplist so the >=2 content-word overlap is genuinely topical (not "the"/"is" agreement).
105	_GROUND_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
106	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "of", "in", "on", "for", "to", "with", "by", "at", "from",
107	    "as", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being", "this", "that", "these", "those",
108	    "it", "its", "their", "his", "her", "our", "your", "we", "they", "he", "she", "you",
109	    "not", "no", "but", "if", "then", "than", "so", "such", "into", "over", "under", "about",
110	    "which", "who", "whom", "whose", "what", "when", "where", "while", "also", "may", "can",
111	    "will", "would", "could", "should", "has", "have", "had", "more", "most", "some", "any",
112	})

115	def _strip_markers(text: str) -> str:
116	    """Remove bracketed ``[N]`` / ``[confidence:...]`` markers so number/word extraction reads the
117	    prose, not the citation index (``[1]`` must never be counted as the decimal ``1``)."""
118	    return _MARKER_RE.sub(" ", text or "")
119	
120	
121	def _strip_confidence_markers(text: str) -> str:
122	    """Remove any ``[confidence:...]`` marker(s) from ``text`` (leaving ``[N]`` citations intact) so a
123	    pre-labeled sentence is re-screened on its bare prose in PROMOTE mode. PURE."""
124	    return _CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE.sub("", text or "").strip()
125	
126	
127	def _decimals(text: str) -> "set[str]":
128	    """The distinct decimal number tokens in ``text`` (comma thousands-separators normalized out)."""
129	    return {d.replace(",", "") for d in _DECIMAL_RE.findall(text or "")}

131	
132	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> "set[str]":
133	    """Lower-cased content tokens (>=3 chars, not a stopword)."""
134	    return {w for w in _WORD_RE.findall(str(text or "").lower())
135	            if len(w) >= 3 and w not in _GROUND_STOPWORDS}

138	def _span_grounds_sentence(sentence: str, span: str) -> bool:
139	    """Deterministic span-grounding leg of the D3 frozen-engine gate (strict_verify §9.1.3 principle).
140	
141	    True iff the sentence's prose is anchored in the cited span: (a) EVERY decimal in the sentence
142	    appears in the span (a synthesis sentence must not invent or alter a number the cited source did

696	                # quote slice — is not falsely dropped (box2 over-drop of the cross-source analytical layer).

698	                grounded = bool(_bspan.strip()) and _span_grounds_sentence(base, _bspan)
699	            else:
700	                grounded = (
701	                    has_span
702	                    and supported.get(i, False)
703	                    and _span_grounds_sentence(base, spans[i])
704	                    and _frozen_engine_verifies_sentence(base, cited_rows_per_sentence[i])
705	                )
706	            if grounded:
707	                marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_MODERATE)
708	                out_sentences.append(f"{base} {marker}")
709	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_promoted_count"] += 1
710	            elif _disclose_analyst and _is_disclosable_interpretation(base, _pool_surnames):

712	                # PURELY-QUALITATIVE interpretive sentence (the analytical connective tissue the composer is
713	                # DESIGNED to write uncited) renders under the analyst-layer DISCLOSURE instead of dropping.
714	                # A fabrication (uncited + a specific number, or a named study absent from the pool) does NOT
715	                # reach here — _is_disclosable_interpretation returns False for it — so it still drops below.
716	                marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_NO_SOURCE)
717	                out_sentences.append(f"{base} {marker}")

719	                    telemetry.get("synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count", 0) + 1
720	                )
721	            else:
722	                dropped.append(sentence)
723	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_dropped_count"] += 1

726	        # ── LEGACY ADVISORY KEEP-and-LABEL (promote OFF, default) — byte-identical ────────────────

730	            out_sentences.append(sentence)
731	            continue
732	        if not has_span:
733	            # No resolvable cited [N] span at all -> a genuinely uncited / unresolvable claim.
734	            marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_NO_SOURCE)
735	            out_sentences.append(f"{sentence} {marker}")
736	            telemetry["synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count"] += 1
737	            continue
738	        if supported.get(i, False):
739	            out_sentences.append(sentence)  # supported -> KEEP unchanged
740	            continue

742	        marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_LOW)
743	        out_sentences.append(f"{sentence} {marker}")
744	        telemetry["synthesis_deviation_labeled_count"] += 1
745	
746	    if dropped:
747	        # Fail-LOUD: the dropped sentences never reach the scored body — surface them so a D3 drop is
748	        # visible in the run log (the typed non-answer audit trail), never a silent deletion.
749	        logger.warning(

751	            "from the scored body (retained only in this audit log, never rendered as a body claim): %s",
752	            len(dropped), dropped,
753	        )
754	    return " ".join(out_sentences), telemetry

seams.concern: strict_verify gate | the PRODUCTION admission predicate
seams.what_it_does: THIS is the production verifier — provenance_generator.py:2548-2559 states it explicitly: 'the production sweep at scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py uses THIS verifier, not clinical_generator/strict_verify'. It appends strings to a `failures` list across ~12 legs and the admission predicate is a single line at 2930: `is_verified = len(failures) == 0`. There is NO score, NO threshold, NO partial credit — one failure string kills the sentence. Legs, in order: no_provenance_token (2186-2193, fires when parse_provenance_tokens() returns []); evidence_not_in_pool / span_out_of_bounds / span_invalid / fetch_shell_cited_span (2209-2240); empty_or_contentless_sentence (2255-2269); number_not_in_any_cited_span (2302-2308); no_integer_overlap_any_cited_span (2314-2335); percent_not_in_cited_span (2351-2371); binding_qualifier_dropped (2410-2427); unsegmentable_script_no_grounding (2455-2457); no_content_word_overlap_any_cited_span (2458-2521, MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP shared content words vs the cited-span aggregate); trial_name_mismatch (2528-2546); entailment_failed (2560-2783); B16 overstatement legs — epistemic / temporal / unit-conflation / S5 numeric-qualifier / L2 numeric-role / L1 clinical-qualifier / L3 polarity (2794-2847); entailment_judge_error_fail_closed (2903-2923).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: An interpretive sentence like 'this discrepancy may reflect adoption lags' dies TWICE over, at two independent legs, before anything subtle happens. (1) If the writer emits it WITHOUT a [#ev:] token — the natural form for a cross-source inference, because no single span supports it — line 2186-2193 returns is_verified=False with failure_reasons=['no_provenance_token'] IMMEDIATELY, before any judge, any overlap check, any rescue. Unlike the clinical lane (see next seam) there is NO is_synthesis_claim exemption in this function: the docstring at 2187 is literally 'Sentences without provenance are rejected outright.' (2) If the writer DOES attach tokens to both sources to satisfy the 'every sentence must end with an [ev_XXX] marker' prompt rule, the sentence survives the mechanical legs (the two spans do share content words with it) and reaches the entailment judge — which is where the cross-source part gets killed. See the entailment_judge seam. Either way it never becomes is_verified=True, and only is_verified=True sentences are ever rendered.

seams.concern: strict_verify gate | the cross-source CONCATENATION — the single most important line for this question
seams.what_it_does: The system does NOT check a sentence against each span separately. Line 2576 is `combined_span = " ".join(aggregated_span_text)` — it CONCATENATES every cited span into one blob and asks the judge ONE question about the blob: `verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)` (2586-2588). So a two-source inference IS given both sources at once. It is not structurally impossible for it to pass — it is semantically refused. The drop is at 2616-2621: `if mode == "enforce": failures.append(f"entailment_failed:{ev_ids}:verdict={verdict}:reason={reason[:80]}")`.
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: This is the crux, and it is NOT what you would expect. The concat means the judge is not blind to the second source — the sentence loses on MERIT, not on plumbing. 'US productivity rose 2.1% [#ev:a] while EU rose 0.4% [#ev:b]' — a pure CONJUNCTION of two spans — passes: every assertion in it is literally in the blob (and section-prompt rule #4 at multi_section_generator.py:3759 explicitly instructs this form: 'If evidence disagrees, say so: one source reports X [ev_001] while another reports Y [ev_002]'). But add ONE interpretive clause — '...a gap that may reflect slower EU adoption' — and the sentence now asserts a CAUSAL MECHANISM that appears in NEITHER span, so the judge returns NEUTRAL and line 2618 appends entailment_failed. The gate's boundary is exactly: CONJUNCTION of two sources = admitted; INFERENCE from two sources = killed. The verifier has no concept of 'this claim is supported by A AND B taken together in a way neither states'; the union of two spans is treated as one bigger span, and a claim that is not IN that bigger span is unsupported by construction.

seams.concern: strict_verify gate | the entailment PREDICATE itself (the sentence that kills synthesis, verbatim)
seams.what_it_does:
The admission predicate is a prompt, not code. Verbatim:

  ENTAILED: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the SPAN. Conservative paraphrase is allowed.
  NEUTRAL: the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN (e.g. SPAN says "GLP-1 RAs", SENTENCE says "semaglutide"; SPAN says "adipocyte metabolism", SENTENCE adds "lipid metabolism" or "energy storage"; SPAN has numbers but not the specific claim being made).
  CONTRADICTED: the SENTENCE asserts something the SPAN explicitly disagrees with.

Judge = z-ai/glm-5.2 (line 106), temperature=0.0 (line 833), reasoning effort 'high'. Default mode is enforce (strict_verify.py:355 `_DEFAULT_MODE = "enforce"`, strict_verify.py:358-379 `_entailment_mode()` reads PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT with that default).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: Read the NEUTRAL clause as the operative rule: 'the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, MECHANISM, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN'. An interpretive sentence is BY DEFINITION the introduction of a mechanism not present in the span — that is what makes it interpretation rather than restatement. 'This discrepancy may reflect adoption lags' introduces the mechanism 'adoption lags' and the causal relation 'reflect'; neither is in the concatenated span. The judge is instructed to call that NEUTRAL. It does not matter that the inference is CORRECT, well-reasoned, or obviously true to a human — the predicate is 'is every assertion present in the span', not 'is this a sound inference'. There is no ENTAILED-BY-INFERENCE verdict in the enum. The three-way enum is the ceiling: a sentence that a reader would call insight is, to this judge, definitionally NEUTRAL. Note the escape hatches are all closed by default: 'off' and 'warn' modes exist (strict_verify.py:527-528, 570) but default is enforce; and a judge TRANSPORT error also drops (entailment_judge_error_fail_closed, provenance_generator.py:2919-2923 — 'FAIL CLOSED ... the unverified sentence is DROPPED').

seams.concern: strict_verify gate | the NEUTRAL rescue, and why it does NOT rescue cross-source inference
seams.what_it_does: A NEUTRAL is not immediately fatal (a CONTRADICTED is — see the U29 note at 2607-2615, CONTRADICTED 'ALWAYS fails closed here'). On NEUTRAL the code hunts for a bounded <=400-char LOCAL WINDOW inside ONE cited row's full direct_quote (`_find_local_content_window` / `_find_local_support_window`, 2654-2710) and RE-JUDGES the sentence against that window alone (2716-2720). If the re-judge returns ENTAILED, the sentence is KEPT (and the token is re-pointed to the rescue window, 2758-2771). If no window exists, or the re-judge is again NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED, it appends entailment_failed (2741-2749, 2772-2783).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: This is the one place you might hope a synthesis sentence gets a second chance — and it is precisely the wrong shape for it. The rescue loop is `for tok in tokens:` with a `break` on the first hit (2654, 2692, 2710): it searches ONE evidence row at a time and re-judges against a window drawn from a SINGLE source. So the second judge call sees STRICTLY LESS cross-source context than the first one did — the first call had both spans concatenated and said NEUTRAL; the rescue call has one source's 400-char window. A claim that fuses A and B cannot be entailed by A alone. The rescue is designed to fix SPAN IMPRECISION (the writer cited bytes 100-140 when the supporting text was at bytes 300-500 of the same row — see the log message at 2751-2756, 'span_imprecise but locally grounded'), not to fix INFERENCE. For a genuine two-source inference, the rescue is a guaranteed second NEUTRAL, i.e. a second drop.

seams.concern: strict_verify gate | the clinical-lane twin, and the ONE synthesis exemption in the codebase
seams.what_it_does: The parallel per-sentence verifier for the clinical_generator lane. Same six-check structure, but returns a `(bool, DropReason|None)` tuple instead of a failure list, with an early return per check: no_provenance_token (421), invalid_token/span_out_of_range (429-436), numeric_mismatch (447-448), percent_not_in_cited_span (461-468), binding_qualifier_dropped (484-495), unsegmentable_script (505-506), empty_or_contentless_sentence (517-518), overlap_too_low (519-521, threshold default 2 content words), entailment_failed (565-571). Line 573 `return True, None` is the ONLY admission point in the function.
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis:
This lane contains the single explicit synthesis carve-out in the whole verify engine, at 417-421:

    tokens = extract_tokens(sentence_text)
    if not tokens:
        if is_synthesis_claim:
            return True, None
        return False, "no_provenance_token"

An untokened sentence PASSES if the caller flags is_synthesis_claim=True. But this is a dead letter for the flywheel report path, for three reasons. (a) The comment at 406-409 says synthesis claims 'pass without provenance BY DEFINITION (I-f5-006)' — it is an assertion by the caller, not a check; nothing verifies the synthesis. (b) VerifiedSentence enforces that a synthesis claim carry ZERO tokens and ALWAYS pass (verified_report.py:177-196: 'is_synthesis_claim=True requires verifier_pass=True', 'requires provenance_tokens=[]'), so it is structurally an UNGATED lane, not a differently-gated one. (c) Crucially, NOTHING in the report generator sets it: the only producer in the entire src/ tree is polaris_v6/api/artifact_to_slice_chain.py:379, an artifact→slice adapter, and it derives the flag from already-computed state (`is_synthesis_claim=len(air_sent.tokens) == 0 and bool(air_sent.is_verified)`). The production writer path (multi_section_generator → strict_verify → verify_sentence_provenance) never calls this function at all, and verify_sentence_provenance has no equivalent exemption. So the escape hatch for un-provenanced synthesis EXISTS in the type system and is UNREACHABLE from the report pipeline.

seams.concern: outline/section structure | dropped sentences are structurally unrenderable
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: There is no third bucket. No 'kept with a caveat', no 'kept at low confidence', no 'flagged for review'. `dropped` is a list that exists only for telemetry and the audit log — it is never re-joined into prose. Combined with the render seam below, an unverified interpretive sentence is not weakened, hedged, or footnoted; it ceases to exist in the output. (One narrow exception exists and proves the rule: strict_verify.py:553-563 in the CLINICAL lane can return `True, "entailment_unverified_judge_error"` — kept-with-a-disclosed-label — but ONLY for a TRANSPORT fault, only under the default-OFF PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE, and the comment at 546-548 is explicit that 'genuine NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED below stay DROPPED (faithfulness NOT relaxed)'. The keep-with-label mechanism exists; it is deliberately not wired to the synthesis case.)

seams.concern: writer prompt / render | the section body is rebuilt ONLY from kept sentences
seams.what_it_does:
The rendered section prose is regenerated from the surviving SentenceVerification objects:

    verified_text, biblio_slice, resolved_emitted = resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count(
        report.kept_sentences, evidence_pool, ...)

The LLM's original draft text is DISCARDED. Only `report.kept_sentences` is reassembled into the [N]-cited body. If nothing survives, 6855-6857 replaces the body with `_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE` (`is_gap_stub = resolved_emitted == 0 or not verified_text.strip()`).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: This is the seam that makes the drop IRREVERSIBLE, and it explains a behavior worth calling out: the writer can write a beautiful interpretive paragraph and the reader will never see one word of it. The report is not the model's draft minus bad sentences — it is a rebuild from the survivor list. Note the failure mode this creates: a section whose evidence supports only synthesis (no directly-quotable findings) renders a GAP STUB rather than an interpretation. The pipeline's terminal verdict `abort_no_verified_sections` (clinical_generator/verified_report.py:38) is the report-level version of the same posture: an unverifiable report is aborted, never shipped hedged.

seams.concern: kept_fraction | NOT a gate — it triggers a rewrite, not a drop
seams.what_it_does: `kept_fraction = report.total_kept / total` (6565) and `post_filter_fraction = post_filter_kept / max(1, report.total_in)` (6579). The ONLY consumer is the retry branch at 6589-6601: `if ... post_filter_fraction < min_kept_fraction and report.total_in > 0:` → log 'below min — retrying' → re-call the LLM with tighter_retry=True and re-verify. `min_kept_fraction` defaults to 0.5 (9779). The retry's output replaces the first pass only if it yields MORE post-filter kept sentences (6649).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: Correcting a likely misconception in the question: kept_fraction does NOT admit or kill any sentence. It is a section-level QUALITY SIGNAL that says 'the writer produced too much unsupported prose — make it write again, more conservatively'. Its effect on synthesis is INDIRECT but real and worth naming: a writer that produces interpretive prose gets most of it dropped → kept_fraction falls below 0.5 → the section is regenerated with the TIGHTER prompt → the second draft hugs the spans harder. So the system doesn't just delete synthesis, it applies selection pressure AGAINST writing it: the feedback loop actively trains the writer (within a run) toward extractive prose. (There is also an `outline_revise.py:71` kept_fraction field feeding outline revision — the same signal escalated to the outline layer.)

seams.concern: evidence assignment / synthesis lane | THE deliberate cross-source lane exists, and is OFF by default
seams.what_it_does:
There IS a lane built expressly for the case you are asking about, and its own source comments diagnose the problem in your exact terms. analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:162-167:

    # synthesis-mode basket gate. The extractive PROMOTE gate requires each synthesis sentence to be
    # ENTAILED/verbatim-grounded by its cited spans — correct for extractive body claims, WRONG for
    # cross-source analytical SYNTHESIS that fuses+interprets sources into a new insight no single span
    # entails (box2: 79/81 real cited cross-source sentences dropped). ... Default OFF.

When PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_MODE=1, the entailment leg is REPLACED (689-698) by a deterministic anti-fabrication floor only: `grounded = bool(_bspan.strip()) and _span_grounds_sentence(base, _bspan)` — every number in the sentence must appear in the UNION of the cited sources' full text, plus >=2 content-word overlap (_span_grounds_sentence, 138-152). NLI entailment is dropped entirely. When PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSED_KEEP=1, an UNCITED interpretive sentence is ALSO kept (710-720) if `_is_disclosable_interpretation` (264-302) holds: no decimals, no 'percent', no crisp quantitative claim (_QUANT_CLAIM_RE), no author attribution absent from the pool, and — for a non-header — it MUST match _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE (219-226: `suggest|impl(y|ies)|may|might|could|consistent with|likely|reflect\w*|mechanism\w*|framework\w*|...`). It is rendered with a BUCKET_NO_SOURCE confidence marker under an ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE preamble.
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: Your example sentence is almost a unit test for this predicate. 'This discrepancy may reflect adoption lags' → no decimals, no 'percent', no _QUANT_CLAIM_RE hit, no author names, and it matches _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE on BOTH 'may' AND 'reflect' → _is_disclosable_interpretation returns True → it would be KEPT with a [confidence: no-source] marker. So the sentence survives in EXACTLY ONE configuration and dies everywhere else. And that configuration is off three times over by default: (1) PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED defaults '0' (155-159); (2) PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_MODE defaults '0' (171-173) and requires promote; (3) PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSED_KEEP defaults '0' (202-203) and requires basket mode. Worse, the whole layer is short-circuited upstream: analyst_synthesis.py:685-692 is an EMITTER-level fail-closed that returns ('', 0, 0) — omitting the entire synthesis block — whenever the layer is on but the PROMOTE gate is off ('refusing UNGATED synthesis (verified core only)'). PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS itself defaults to '1' (analyst_synthesis.py:672), so in the stock configuration the layer runs, immediately hits the emitter fail-closed, and emits nothing. NET: the synthesis lane is present, thought-through, empirically motivated (its comments cite a measured 79/81 and 31-sentence over-drop), and DARK by default. The capability is built and disabled.

seams.concern: dedup | 'B11' is not a verify gate at all
seams.what_it_does: B11 in this codebase is the SAME-SPAN DEDUP pass, not span-grounding. It collapses degenerate restatements to one rendering per distinct resolved-span FOOTPRINT — the (ev_id, start, end) SET a sentence cites — keep-first. The motivating defect (docstring, 159-163): 'the legacy section producer emitted ONE verified span as 18 near-identical sentences — 18x the SAME footprint, zero added breadth'. It runs AFTER strict_verify, on report.kept_sentences (6772-6774).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: Flagging this as a correction to the question's framing: there is no 'B11 span-grounding gate'. B11 is a post-verify redundancy filter. But it does encode a philosophy that bears on synthesis, stated in its own docstring: 'repetition of the SAME span is NOT corroboration; corroboration is DISTINCT works (distinct ev_ids).' So the system's model of cross-source reasoning is CITATION-COUNTING (distinct ev_ids = breadth), not INFERENCE (fusing sources = insight). B11 rewards a sentence that cites A and B for the same fact; the entailment gate kills a sentence that concludes something FROM A and B. Those two mechanisms together define the system's ceiling precisely: it can corroborate across sources, and it cannot reason across them.

input.notes:
DIRECT ANSWER TO THE QUESTION ASKED.

Where a sentence is admitted or killed — there are TWO verifiers, and the one people cite is not the one that runs.

- PRODUCTION path: `verify_sentence_provenance` at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:2098-2959. Its own comment at :2548-2559 says so outright: "the production sweep at scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py uses THIS verifier, not clinical_generator/strict_verify". Driver: `strict_verify` (same file, :3598-3759), partitioning at :3753-3759.
- CLINICAL path: `verify_sentence` at src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:386-573. Same six checks, different return shape. Not on the flywheel report path.

THE ADMISSION PREDICATE, quoted:

  provenance_generator.py:2930      is_verified = len(failures) == 0
  provenance_generator.py:3756-3759 if v.is_verified: kept.append(v)
                                    else:             dropped.append(v)

That is the whole thing. ~12 legs each append a string to `failures`; one string is fatal. No score, no threshold, no partial credit. (The clinical twin's equivalent is a single `return True, None` at strict_verify.py:573 reached only if every early-return is skipped.)

WHAT HAPPENS TO AN INFERENCE ACROSS TWO SOURCES — the answer is more interesting than "no single span supports it, so it dies".

The verifier does NOT check spans one at a time. provenance_generator.py:2576 concatenates them:

    combined_span = " ".join(aggregated_span_text)
    verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)   # :2586-2588

So the judge SEES both sources together. The cross-source sentence loses on merit, not on plumbing. What kills it is the NEUTRAL clause of the judge prompt (llm/entailment_judge.py:588), quoted verbatim:

    NEUTRAL: the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN

An interpretation is BY DEFINITION the introduction of a mechanism not present in the span — that is what makes it interpretation rather than restatement. "This discrepancy may reflect adoption lags" introduces the mechanism "adoption lags" and the causal relation "reflect"; neither is in the concatenated span. Verdict NEUTRAL → provenance_generator.py:2618 appends `entailment_failed` → is_verified=False → dropped. Correctness of the inference is irrelevant; there is no ENTAILED-BY-INFERENCE verdict in the three-way enum. Default mode is enforce (strict_verify.py:355 `_DEFAULT_MODE = "enforce"`).

The gate's boundary is therefore exact and worth stating plainly:
  CONJUNCTION of two sources → ADMITTED. ("US rose 2.1% [#ev:a] while EU rose 0.4% [#ev:b]" — every assertion is in the blob. The section prompt at multi_section_generator.py:3759 explicitly TEACHES this form.)
  INFERENCE from two sources → KILLED. (Add "...a gap that may reflect slower EU adoption" and the same sentence now dies.)

If the writer instead emits the inference UNTOKENED (the natural form, since no span supports it), it dies even earlier and more bluntly — provenance_generator.py:2186-2193, docstring "Sentences without provenance are rejected outright", failure_reasons=['no_provenance_token'], before any judge runs.

THE NEUTRAL RESCUE DOES NOT HELP (and I want to be clear I checked). A NEUTRAL is not immediately fatal — :2622-2783 hunts a <=400-char window in ONE cited row and re-judges. But the loop is `for tok in tokens:` with a `break` on first hit (:2654, :2692, :2710), so the second judge call sees a window from a SINGLE source — strictly LESS cross-source context than the first call that already said NEUTRAL. The rescue is built for SPAN IMPRECISION (writer cited the wrong bytes of the right row; see the log line at :2751-2756), not for inference. For a genuine two-source inference it is a guaranteed second NEUTRAL, i.e. a second drop.

THE DROP IS IRREVERSIBLE. The rendered section is REBUILT from survivors — multi_section_generator.py:6802-6808 passes `report.kept_sentences` to `resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count`; the LLM's draft text is discarded. There is no "kept with a caveat" bucket. Zero survivors → a gap stub (:6855-6857). Report-level, the terminal verdict is `abort_no_verified_sections` (clinical_generator/verified_report.py:38): an unverifiable report is aborted, never shipped hedged.

THINGS THE QUESTION ASSUMED THAT ARE NOT TRUE — reporting these plainly, as instructed.

1. "kept_fraction" is NOT an admission gate. It is a section QUALITY signal (multi_section_generator.py:6565, 6579) whose only consumer is a RETRY branch (:6589-6601, `min_kept_fraction` default 0.5 at :9779). It kills no sentence. But it has a real second-order effect on synthesis worth knowing: heavy interpretive prose → mass drops → kept_fraction < 0.5 → section REGENERATED with the tighter prompt → the second draft hugs the spans harder. The system doesn't merely delete synthesis, it applies within-run selection pressure against WRITING it.

2. "B11" is NOT span-grounding. In this codebase B11 is the same-span DEDUP pass (verified_compose.py:156-163, called at multi_section_generator.py:6760-6777), collapsing 18x restatements of one span. It runs AFTER verification. Its docstring does however state the system's whole theory of cross-source reasoning: "repetition of the SAME span is NOT corroboration; corroboration is DISTINCT works (distinct ev_ids)." Corroboration = citation counting. Not inference.

3. `is_synthesis_claim` — the one synthesis exemption in the engine — is REACHABLE ONLY IN DEAD CODE for this pipeline. strict_verify.py:417-421 does let an untokened sentence pass if the caller asserts is_synthesis_claim=True, and VerifiedSentence hard-requires such claims to carry zero tokens and always pass (verified_report.py:177-196) — i.e. it is an UNGATED lane, not a differently-gated one. But nothing in the report generator sets it: the sole producer in src/ is polaris_v6/api/artifact_to_slice_chain.py:379, an adapter that back-derives the flag from already-computed state. The production verifier has no equivalent exemption at all.

THE BIGGEST FINDING — the capability is BUILT and DARK.

src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py is a lane written expressly for cross-source synthesis, and its comments diagnose the problem in the same words the question uses (:162-167):

    # The extractive PROMOTE gate requires each synthesis sentence to be ENTAILED/verbatim-grounded
    # by its cited spans — correct for extractive body claims, WRONG for cross-source analytical
    # SYNTHESIS that fuses+interprets sources into a new insight no single span entails
    # (box2: 79/81 real cited cross-source sentences dropped). ... Default OFF.

Under PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_MODE the NLI entailment leg is REPLACED by a deterministic anti-fabrication floor only (:689-698): every number must appear in the UNION of the cited sources' FULL TEXT, plus >=2 content-word overlap. Under PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSED_KEEP an UNCITED interpretive sentence is kept with a [confidence: no-source] marker under a disclosure preamble (:710-720) if it passes `_is_disclosable_interpretation` (:264-302): no decimals, no "percent", no crisp quantitative claim, no author attribution absent from the pool, and it must match _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE (:219-226).

Your example sentence is nearly a unit test for that predicate: "this discrepancy may reflect adoption lags" has no numbers, no percent, no fabricated names, and matches _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE on BOTH "may" and "reflect" → it would be KEPT and disclosed. So the sentence survives in EXACTLY ONE configuration and dies in every other.

That configuration is off FOUR times over:
  - PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED default "0"  (deviation_check.py:155-159)
  - PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_MODE       default "0"  (:171-173, requires promote)
  - PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSED_KEEP    default "0"  (:202-203, requires basket)
  - and the layer is short-circuited upstream regardless: analyst_synthesis.py:685-692 is an EMITTER-level fail-closed returning ('', 0, 0) — omitting the ENTIRE synthesis block — whenever the layer is on but PROMOTE is off ("refusing UNGATED synthesis (verified core only)"). PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS itself defaults to "1" (analyst_synthesis.py:672), so in the stock config the layer runs, instantly hits the fail-closed, and emits nothing.

So the honest summary is not "POLARIS cannot do synthesis." It is: POLARIS has a working, empirically-motivated synthesis lane (its own comments cite a measured 79/81 cited-sentence and 31 interpretive-sentence over-drop), and it is disabled by default behind four flags and an emitter guard. The system as shipped can CORROBORATE across sources and cannot REASON across them — and that is a deliberate, documented, reversible choice, not an architectural limit.
